text: Bɛɛ som maŋ bo yakaa, nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan denibalaŋ gbagba be kyaa yɛɛ nɛ, ŋan te bɛɛ mo wola balaŋ beo wɔ nyi ŋe yɛɛ maŋ Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋa.’ ” Kpene keŋ ne yɛɛ walaŋ ayimɛɛ Maake

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”

text: Balaŋ baŋ kena nyi baŋ be nawɔɔ ba yekee ne tɛɛ, te omumuuse ne kolosi te ɔtakasese wose ne yɛɛ wɔ doŋ, te siayɛlɛɛsatena sia ne naa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ. Keŋte ba lese Iseraetena Wurubuarɛ ŋon yele. Yesu kefa balaŋ kakpoŋse banaara weenɛɛ Maake

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Keŋte Yesu yela balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee tɛɛle man.

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ ka gyoo Kaesaria Filipi tɛɛle man nɛ, ɔ bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Balaŋ ne kolosi ma wose man nombia na be kpa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna amɔ?”

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “De lii balinɔɔ te ŋɔma yasɛɛ na ɛ ne tale naa yako nyi, tɛɛ de naa na weese waa gyɔ.

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Te nebo mɔ na, ɛ ne tale kɛɛ ŋɔma dinɔɔ yako nyi gyɛŋ ɔga waa ŋɛɛ. Ɛ ne tale kɛɛ ŋɔma na ɛ kawola kpene keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ. Mɔna nombia ŋan ne yɛɛ debaŋ kei man nɛ, ɛ bɛɛ tale wola ŋe man ya.

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ baale kpa nyaŋ yɛna Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon. Baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Elia, te baale mɔ kpa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyeremia, yaa Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ man ŋolo.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: Lee debaŋ kenaŋ pou nɛ, Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na lese nombia ŋan asɛɛ fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Kaboena nyi maa gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man, na me kaa naa diyem nideli Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ nyiŋmaa man. Baa ko maŋ, mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ man na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Keŋ de ta mo bodobodo kɔŋ dɔɔ ya te ɔ ne yako mena nombia kɛŋa yaa?”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon na me kpilala dɛɛ lee adido kɔŋ me kya Wurubuarɛ gyoorobiiri man, na me kaa tɔ walaŋ kamasɛ kom lee ɔ tom keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ man.

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako neŋ nyiaa, nyaŋ yɛna Pita, te n yele kenaŋ asɛɛ yɛna nyi boe. Nyaŋ boe kei dɔɔ te maa baake me balaŋ pou yilaa. Te yeŋ gba koraŋ be gyae ke tale di be dɔɔ ya.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ mɔ de? Ɛ kpa maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna amɔ?”

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Botɔɔ te Simɔn Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ. Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ Bu.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Gyona bu Simɔn, nyaŋ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi na deniwalaŋ lese na nombia kɛŋa wola neŋ ya, mɔna me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ lese na ŋa wola neŋ.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Maa mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ safowaase do n nyiŋmaa man. Kpene kamasɛ keŋ nyaŋ ke gyɔ ke dowoe tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, baa gyɔ ke dowoe adido, te kpene kamasɛ keŋ nyaŋ ke booli ke tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nɛ, baa booli ke adido mɔ.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Keŋte Yesu faree ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi, be na yako walaŋ kamasɛ nyi ŋon yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ya. Yesu kekolosi lee ɔ yeŋ na ɔ kefoŋ wose man Maake

text: Botɔɔ te Pita moo Yesu lee yenaŋ te ɔ tɔɔ e nyi, “Me Gbeŋgyoo, na yako mena ya. Wurubuarɛ dɛɛ mo kɛŋa gyu lem. Kɛŋa na kɔŋ n dɔɔ korakora ya.”

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ Pita te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ɔbɔnsam, ta lee me gyaŋ! N ne gyae n ke tɔ maŋ gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi na Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ ya, mɔna denibalaŋ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ.”

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa sila maŋ na, ɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae ya, mɔna waa mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele, na waa sila maŋ wee kamasɛ.

text: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ? Yaa woŋ te walaŋ waa tale mo kyɛɛkee na ɔ nyeedoŋ?

text: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ gyeŋawɔ nyiaa, Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon wose man nombia yaa ɔ ne yako nɛ. Yesu kekyɔ tobaale ŋon ɔ na wee keleuleuse nɛ Maake

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Keŋ tobaale ŋon te nyiŋ kɔba keŋ waa tɔ ya dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako wɔ nyi baa mo ŋon na ɔ wɛɛle na ɔ bia na wa abɔɔ ŋan pou ɔ dana nɛ ke yɔɔ, na baa mo kaa tɔ kom keŋ.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: Keŋte ɔ tobaale ŋon tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, ‘Nyiŋ konɔɔ fa maŋ, maa tɔ neŋ kom keŋ pou.’

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Botɔɔ te ɔ nombia yɛɛ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon waraŋase, te ɔ moo kom keŋ pou kyɛɛ e te ɔ tina e yela nyi waa ta.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: Keŋte ɔ dɔɔ ŋon mɔ tisiwɔ buŋ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ waase e nyiaa, ‘Me dɔɔ nyiŋ konɔɔ fa maŋ maa tɔ neŋ n kom keŋ pou.’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: Mɔna tobaale ŋon bɛe, te ɔ yela ba moo ɔ dɔɔ ŋon ketɔ deni nyi waa doo botɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ waa tɔ e ɔ kom keŋ pou.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Tewulɛɛ akaŋ baŋ kena mena nɛ takaa wɔ nideli, keŋte be gyuuwɔ ke tɔlɛɛ nombia ŋan pou fa be gbeŋgyoo ŋon.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Botɔɔ te be gbeŋgyoo ŋon baake tobaale ŋon te ɔ yako e nyiaa, ‘N yɛɛ walaŋkum ŋolo e. Nyaŋ waase maŋ te ma moo n kom keŋ n dana maŋ nɛ pou kyɛɛ neŋ.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Mena dɔɔ ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon gyeŋa baŋ, te ɔ yela ba moo tobaale ŋon ketɔ deni na waa naa diyem kelii debaŋ keŋ, ŋon mɔ waa tɔ ɔ koma ŋan ɔ dana e nɛ pou.

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ta mo ɛ tebia nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ lee ɛ konɔɔse man ya nɛ, mena te me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waa yɛɛ ŋon.”

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Yesu kekolosi nombia kɛŋa pou taŋ nɛ, ɔ leewɔ Galelia tɛɛle man gyu Gyudia tɛɛle man, Gyɔɔdan boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Balaŋ baale kyaa keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tale yala ala ya, nawolo nyi mena te ba lola wɔ. Balaŋ baale mɔ dɔɔ, balaŋ yɛɛ na wɔ keŋ nyi bɔɔ gyae baa tale yala ala ya. Balaŋ baale mɔ dɔɔ, Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ dɔɔ te baŋ gbagba bɛɛwɔ nyi bɔɔ yale ya. Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa tale lɛɛ nombia kɛŋa do na, waa lɛɛ ŋa do.” Yesu ke hiraa bia Maake

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Balaŋ burum sila e gyu botɔɔ te ɔ kyɔɔ wɔ be kaweese pou.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Keŋte Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ be kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Da mmaraa ne fa daa gbɛɛ nyi ŋolo waa tale bɛɛ ɔ wɛɛle lee nombii kamasɛ dɔɔ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan nyi, lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ alo na baale bo,

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Lee kei dɔɔ nɛ baale waa tina ɔ kya na ɔ naa yela, na waa mo ɔ wose mataa na ɔ wɛɛle, na be balaŋ bala ka bese walaŋ dokoloŋ yee?’

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Mena dɔɔ be ta tekaa yɛɛ balaŋ bala bela ya, mɔna be ta bese walaŋ dokoloŋ. Mena dɔɔ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo bom yɛɛ ke dokoloŋ nɛ, ŋolo na ŋolo na kpase ke man ya.”

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa mena te doo na, weera dɔɔ te de naana Mosesi yɛɛ mmaraa see nyi, baale waa tale ŋmarase yale kebɛɛ tɔne fa ɔ wɛɛle na waa bɛɛ e?”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ konɔɔyuŋ dɔɔ te de naana Mosesi fa ŋon mena gbɛɛ kenaŋ. Mɔna lee kewalaŋ keŋ na, be doo mena ya.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, nyi akpaa na alo lee ɔ baale wɔle bo ya, te ɔ baale de bɛɛ e gyu ke yala alo wɔle nɛ, Wurubuarɛ siaman na ɔtenate gyae ŋolo wɛɛle baa.”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Nombia ŋan mɛɛ yako ŋon nɛ, na walaŋ kamasɛ ne gyae la waa tale lɛɛ ŋa do ya, gyisɛ balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ ba mo mena bɛrɛɛ kenaŋ boele wɔ nɛ baageŋ.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Keŋte balaŋ baale moo bia kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa be dɔɔ na waa fane fa wɔ. Mɔna ɔ kaseela baŋ kena mena nɛ, be ŋmanyaŋ balaŋ baŋ.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Wee daale nɛ tobaale ŋolo kaa bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, woŋ deeli yaa maa yɛɛ na maa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Botɔɔ te tobaale ŋon bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Mmaraase ŋemɔ?” Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Na ko walaŋ ya, na lee n wɛɛle yaa n baale wɔle ya, na ŋmɛɛle ya, na kara n dɔɔ nɔɔ ya.

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: Yesu ka mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa bia baŋ dɔɔ taŋ nɛ, te ɔ leewɔ botɔɔ laŋ. Kɔbate ŋolo wose man nombia Maake

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Weera te n ne bɔɔse maŋ lee deeli keyɛɛ wose man. Wurubuarɛ waageŋ dei la. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa n ne gyae n ke nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ nɛ, kenaŋ na kaboena nyi nya di Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan dɔɔ.”

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Yesu kena mena nɛ ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela bia baŋ ke kɔŋ me gyaŋ. Ɛ na tɔ wɔ gbɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ yɔkɔse ba wose ŋgba bia kɛwɔ dinɔɔ nɛ wui.”

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Mɔna balaŋ burum baŋ be yɛɛ siamantenawɔ nɛ, baa kaa bese wɔle nyibaŋ, na balaŋ burum baŋ be yɛɛ wɔle nyibaŋwɔ nɛ kaa bese siamantena.”

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Bu n kya na n naa, na n ke gyae n tebia ŋgba mena keŋ n ne gyae nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.”

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Keŋte tobaale ŋon yakowɔ nyiaa, “Me wulaa mee di mmaraase kɛŋa pou dɔɔ. Woŋ kaa la nyi maa yɛɛ bela?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Nyi akpaa n ne gyae n ke de kelii ɔto na, gyu ke yɔɔ nya abɔɔ ŋan pou n dana nɛ, na n ka mo kɔba keŋ kpɛlɛɛ ayematena na n ke kɔŋ kaa sila maŋ. N yɛɛ mena na, n ke nyiŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa see n wose Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ adido.”

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Tobaale ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ moo waraŋase lee botɔɔ nawolo nyi ɔ dana kɔba na nsendee ya.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Mɛɛ yako ŋon bela nyiaa, gyae ke yɛɛ doŋ fa baŋ be dana kɔba nɛ nyi baa gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi kurumɔɔ ka gyoo ŋarale bɔɔ man lee.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Yesu kaseela baŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ doo be nɔɔ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyi nnɛ te doo na, amɔte ne gyae la waa nyiŋ nyeekelɛɛ baa?”

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Keŋte Yesu kɛɛ wɔ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Denibalaŋ gyaŋ na ŋolo be tale yɛɛ nombii kei ya, mɔna kpene kamasɛ be kyaa keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tale yɛɛ ya.”

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, debaŋ keŋ kpene kamasɛ de kaa bese wɔle te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon de kyaa me gyooro gbeli keŋ dɔɔ me gyoorobiiri man nɛ, ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ be sila maŋ nɛ ɛ ke kyaa gyooro gbelɛɛ kufu ala dɔɔ, na ɛ ke di Iseraetena akpaŋalaŋse kufu ala ŋan balaŋ nombia.

ref: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
text: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ yaa ɔ naabia yaa ɔ deesina, yaa ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa yaa ɔ bia yaa ɔ tɛɛlese yela lee maŋ dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ŋa akpeŋ sɔŋonoŋ (

ref: times as much. They will also have eternal life.
text: ) kpu, na waa bese nyiŋ nyeedoŋ kekpaa keŋ mɔ.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: Keŋte Yesu gyɔɔ dudu lese nombia ŋan ɔ be yako nɛ man wola wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba wɔɔfarale ŋon, ɔ ba koro nebo nebo fuu gyu ɔ ne gyae paasetena na be kaa yɛɛ tom ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Balee ŋon na paasetena baŋ pou ke sɛɛ wee dokoloŋ koya dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ moo wɔ gyu ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man nyi ba ke yɛɛ tom.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: Kegyɔ debaŋ kadaale nɛ, ɔ leewɔ gyu abɔntere man te ɔ kenaa tewulɛɛ baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yakaa bɔɔ ne yɛɛ kolo ya.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa ‘Ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyu me wɔɔ keŋ man ke yɛɛ tom. Maa tɔ ŋon kom keŋ kaboena nyi maa tɔ ŋon nɛ.’ ”

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
text: Mena dɔɔ baŋ mɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man. Weese ka seŋ dɛɛ, na ɔ ke gɛɛse nɛ, ɔ besewɔ ka mo balaŋ gyu ɔ wɔɔ keŋ man bela.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Ke lii balinɔɔ weese ŋgba debaŋ anoŋ nɛ, ɔ besewɔ gyu kena balaŋ baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yakaa. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera te gyɛŋ pou ɛ seŋɛɛ kɛbo yakaa ɛ bɛɛ yɛɛ kolo ya?”

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Nawolo nyi ŋolo te fa daa tom dɛɛ yɛɛ ya.” Botɔɔ te balee ŋon yako wɔ nyiaa, “Kenaŋ na ɛmɛɛ mɔ ɛ gyu ke yɛɛ tom me wɔɔman.”

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: Ke lii balinɔɔ nɛ, wɔɔte ŋon yako ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ man kegyia ŋon nyiaa, “Baake paasetena baŋ na n ke tɔ wɔ kom. Taŋgbɛɛ tɔ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ kom, pɛna n kaa tɔ baŋ bɔɔ taŋgbɛɛ gyu nɛ.”

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle gyu wɔɔ keŋ man balinɔɔ weese nɛ kɔŋawɔ, te be tɔɔ wɔ kom ŋgba mena keŋ wɔɔte ŋon be yako nyi waa tɔ gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ nɛ.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Ba ke lɛɛ be kɔbase taŋ nɛ, be fiasɛɛ bɛɛ kolosi tia wɔɔte ŋon nyiaa,

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: “Balaŋ kɛwɔ baŋ bɔɔ kɔŋ wɔle wɔle kei nɛ, yɛɛ tom bo debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ. Mɔna daa dɔɔ da seŋawɔ weese kpalala kei man leenaŋ nebo kaa lii balinɔɔ kei. Kenaŋ wɔle koraŋ na, n besewɔ tɔ de popou kom kyɛɛkyɛɛ.”

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: Botɔɔ te wɔɔte ŋon tiranɔɔ fa be man ŋolo nyiaa, “Me gyoo, me ta ŋere neŋ do ya na. Ŋgba daa na neŋ kolosiwɔ see nyi n yɛɛ tom wee dibiliŋ na, nnɛ te maa tɔ neŋ kom.

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Mo n kɔba na n ke ta gyu n dɛɛ. Maŋ gbagba gyae la nyi maa tɔ baŋ bɔɔ dɔɔ wɔle kɔŋ nɛ kom ŋgba mena keŋ mɔɔ tɔ neŋ nɛ.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Me dana gbɛɛ keŋ nyi maa mo maŋ gbagba me kɔba yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae. Keŋ me dana wɔe dɔɔ te na wee neŋ nɛ?”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena mɔ te wɔle nyibaŋ baa kaa bese gyaŋgba nyibaŋ, na gyaŋgba nyibaŋ mɔ kaa bese wɔle nyibaŋ.” Yesu ka bese kolosi lee ɔ yeŋ wose man Maake

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu dɔɔwɔ seŋ te ɔ baake wɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa ŋon?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu wii be waraŋase, te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tinaa be sia. Debokenaŋ man te be sia gyuusuwɔ, te be sila Yesu laŋ.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ ŋmanyaŋ wɔ nyi baa wu be nɔɔ. Mɔna nɛnɛɛ gba te be desina fae yeesa nedoŋ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.”

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Wee daale Yesu kee gyu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ baake ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ lee yenaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa,

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: Baa mo maŋ do balaŋ baŋ ba te yɛɛ Gyudatenawɔ ya nɛ nyiŋmaa man na baa kulu maŋ, na baa tuku maŋ na baa gyɔ maŋ sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ na maa yeŋ. Mɔna weeya atoorote na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.” Sebedeo bia Maake

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Keŋte Sebedeo bia, Gyemisi na Gyɔn be naa moo wɔ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ sola e nyi waa kyɔ e na nombii daale.

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Woŋ te n ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ fa neŋ?” Te alo ŋon tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae nyi n ke fa me bia bala kɛwɔ gbɛɛ, na ŋolo ke kyaa n dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ŋolo mɔ ke kyaa n demena dɔɔ n gyoori keŋ man.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ba gyeŋ kpene ɛ na solo nɛ ya. Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ?” Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyi, “Ayi, dɛɛ tale.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ke tale naa diyem keŋ maa naa nɛ, mɔna mɔɔ taŋ doŋ keŋ maa lese walaŋ ŋon waa kyaa ma dunoluŋ yaa ma demena dɔɔ ya. Mena akyaese kɛŋa doe fa balaŋ baŋ me kya gbagba be desina see wɔ nɛ.”

ref: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
text: Yesu kaseela kufu akaŋ baŋ ke nyii mena nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Gyemisi na Gyɔn dɔɔ.

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ pou yilaa te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyeŋ nyi tɛɛle kei dɔɔ gyooneŋ ne di gyoori bo be balaŋ dɔɔ, te be kegyiise baŋ mɔ dana doŋ be dɔɔ.

ref: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
text: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, na mena te kaboena nyi dɛɛ doo ɛ man ya. Ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ kegyia nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ kegbetibii.

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
text: Te ɛ man walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae nyi waa bese ɛ siamante nɛ, kaboena nyi waa bese ɛ popou ɛ kegbetibii.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: Mena mɔ te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon te kɔŋ nyi balaŋ baa som maŋ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa som balaŋ, na maa yeŋ lɛɛ balaŋ burum nyee.” Yesu kekyɔ siayɛlɛɛsatena bala baale Maake

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kɛɛ lee Gyeriko donɔɔ man nɛ, balaŋ burum sila wɔ.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Na siayɛlɛɛsatena bala baale kyaa gbɛɛnaa nɔɔman botɔɔ. Be ke nyii nyi Yesu ne laŋa laa nɛ, be gyinaa be nɔɔ baake nyiaa “De Gbeŋgyoo, gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii, wii de waraŋase.”

ref: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
text: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, dɛɛ gyae de sia ke gyuusu.”

ref: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Balee ŋolo kyaawɔ keŋ ɔ dana bia baala bala. Wee daale nɛ, ɔ gyuu ɔ bu kegyia ŋon gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, ‘Me bu, mɛɛ gyae nyi gyɛŋ n ke gyu me wɔɔman ke yɛɛ tom fa maŋ.’

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Mɔna paasetena baŋ kena ɔ bu ŋon nɛ be yako dɔŋa nyiaa, ‘Kei yɛna bu ŋon waa kaa di ɔ kya abɔɔ ŋan. Ɛ yela dɛɛ ko e na abɔɔ ŋan nafɔ waa di nɛ ka bese de wui!’

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Akpaa ŋolo de bɔɔse ŋon nombii daale nɛ, ɛ yako e nyiaa, ‘De Gbeŋgyoo Yesu ne gyae na wɔ.’ Ɛ yako mena nɛ, waa tina wɔ yela fa ŋon debokenaŋ man.”

ref: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
text: Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yako Gyerusalɛmtena nyiaa, ‘Ɛ kɛɛ! Ɛ gyoo ne kɔŋ ɛ gyaŋ. Ɔ te yɔkɔse ɔ wose, te ɔ kyaa afurum dɔɔ. Ɔ kyae afurum bu dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ.’ ”

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Keŋte Yesu kaseela baŋ gyuuwɔ ke yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yako wɔ nɛ.

ref: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
text: Ba moo afurum ŋon na ɔ bu kɔŋ, te ba moo be kanyaŋse gyakaa be dɔɔ, te Yesu gyeŋawɔ kyaa.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Yesu ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, donɔɔ keŋ pou siŋiiwɔ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti yɛna kei?”

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
text: Mɔna saese kegyiise na mmaraa wola baŋ kena gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan Yesu be yɛɛ, na mena keŋ bia ne fae yeesa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nyiaa, “Gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii lɛɛ daa” nɛ, ba gyeŋa baŋ Yesu dɔɔ.

ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii Yesu aduya kɛŋa nɛ, ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ba wose man nombia yaa ɔ ne yako.

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ne yako daa nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Me deni gyae ke yɛɛ botɔɔ keŋ baa fane,’ mɔna ɛmɛɛ ta besena ke yɛɛ ŋmɛɛlatena dɛɛgyaŋee.”

ref: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
text: Nyi akpaa ɛ lɛɛ maŋ di nɛ, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ ke fane bɔɔse Wurubuarɛ nɛ, ɛ nyiŋmaa gyae ke kaŋ ke.” Ba ke bɔɔse Yesu ɔ doŋ wose man nombia Maake

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Keŋte Yesu leewɔ be gyaŋ donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ gyuuwɔ Bɛtani donɔɔ man, te ɔ doowɔ botɔɔ. Yesu ke falaa fiigi daŋ keŋ Maake

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ mɔ ne gyae maa bɔɔse ŋon nombii dokoloŋ. Nyi akpaa ɛ tiranɔɔ fa maŋ nɛ kenaŋ na maŋ mɔ maa wola ŋon doŋ keŋ me dana mɛɛ yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou.

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Tɛɛ kena nebo nebo fuu keŋ Yesu ba bese ɔ wɔle ɔ ne kɔŋ Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, na tanam na ko e.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, akpaa ɛ lɛɛ maŋ di nideli te ɛ te lɛɛ aŋmaareŋ ya nɛ, na kpene keŋ be kɔŋ fiigi daŋ kei dɔɔ nɛ kaageŋ te ɛ ke tale yɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ ke tale yako bula kei koraŋ nyiaa, ‘Koro ke yala ɛpo man’ te ke gyae ke kɔŋ mena.

ref: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
text: Mena dɔɔ be tiranɔɔ fa Yesu nyiaa, “Dɔɔ gyeŋ ya.” Keŋte Yesu mɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa mena na, maŋ mɔ ba wola ŋon me doŋ keŋ da lee ya.” Bia bala baale wose maŋ dudu

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ nyii dudu daale bela. Wɔɔfarale ŋolo fara wɔɔ te ɔ duu daasebia ŋaale ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba akutuse te bɛɛ baake ŋa nyi gerepo nɛ. Ɔ dii abaŋ kilisi wɔɔ keŋ, te ɔ wula bɔɔ keŋ baa sara daasebia ŋan ke man na baa lese ŋa loŋ lee ŋe man nɛ. Te ɔ baŋa deni dɔɔloŋ dɔɔloŋ daale, keŋ walaŋ ke kyaa ke man na waa gyee wɔɔ kenaŋ. Te ɔ moo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena baale nyiŋmaa man nyi baa kɛɛ ke dɔɔ, te ɔ korowɔ laŋ gbɛɛ.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, te ɔ kɛɛ wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan nɛ, saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Doŋ woŋti yaa n dana n ne yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou? Amɔte faa na neŋ mena doŋ kenaŋ?”

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋkesɔ keŋ ɔ be sɔ balaŋ nɛ, ka lee aleŋ? Ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ yaa balaŋ gyaŋ?” Botɔɔ te ba gyoo nombia ŋan man te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Woŋ te dɛɛ yako? ‘Akpaa de yako nyi ka lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ na, waa bɔɔse daa nyi woŋ dɔɔ te da te lɛɛ Gyɔn nombia ŋan di ya?’

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Te akpaa de yako nyi ka lee balaŋ gyaŋ na, dee yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ mɔ. Nawolo nyi be popou ne lɛɛ di nideli nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.”

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: Keŋte ɔ bu ŋon yako e nyiaa, mɔɔ gyae maa gyu ya, mɔna wɔle wɔle nɛ, ɔ kaa kyɛɛkee wa agyueŋ te ɔ gyuuwɔ.

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: Keŋte balee ŋon gyuuwɔ ɔ bu kemaasa ŋon gyaŋ ke yako ŋon mɔ mena ke. Ɔ bu kemaasa ŋon sɛɛwɔ nyi, ‘Yoo taata me nyiiwɔ, maa gyu,’ mɔna ɔ te gyu ya.

ref: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ wola maŋ ya agyueŋ. Bia bala kɛwɔ man na, woŋti yɛɛna ɔ kya ŋon kegyaebii? Te balaŋ baŋ tiranɔɔ fae e nyiaa, ‘Bu kegyia ŋon.’ Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, lempoolɛɛra na kafoŋtena te taŋgbɛɛ ŋon bɛɛ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.’

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Nawolo nyi Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon kɔŋawɔ kaa wola ŋon gbɛɛ kpaakpaate keŋ ɛ ka mo, mɔna ɛ te lɛɛ e di ya. Lempoolɛɛra na kafoŋtena baŋ dɔɔ, be lɛɛ e di. Kenaŋ wɔle ɛ kena mena koraŋ nɛ, ɛ te kyɛɛkee ya agyueŋ na ɛ ke lɛɛ e di ya.” Paasetena baale dudu Maake

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Debaŋ kelii nyi baa kɔ daasebia ŋan nɛ, ɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ nyi baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: Botɔɔ te paasetena baŋ kyaŋa ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ, te be tuku ŋolo te ba koo ŋolo te be fuŋii ŋolo mɔ boya.

ref: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
text: Keŋte wɔɔte ŋon besewɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ ba boe kela gyaŋgba nyibaŋ baŋ gyu paasetena baŋ gyaŋ, mɔna be yɛɛ baŋ mɔ mena ke.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, wɔɔte ŋon kpila ŋon gbagba ɔ bu te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, ‘Mberem ŋon na baa bu e.’

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Mena dɔɔ be wɔŋee e lee wɔɔ keŋ man ka ko.

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, Mena dɔɔ wee keŋ wɔɔte ŋon de kɔŋ na, woŋ te waa yɛɛ paasetena baŋ?”

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Waa koe mena balaŋ kumɛɛ benaŋ pou, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena wɔlɛɛ baŋ debaŋ de lii na, baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan na ɛ kena kpene keŋ ne yako nɛ yee? Be ŋmarasewɔ see nyiaa, ‘Boe keŋ deni mmaraa baŋ be bɛɛ nɛ, keŋ kaa bese na mokɔɔ kasee boe keŋ ne yeli deni keŋ ne yɛɛ doŋ. De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ yɛɛ la te kɔŋawɔ mena, te ke yɛɛ kpene keŋ ke dana gyakoloŋ de siaman’ ”

ref: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa lɛo ɔ gyoori keŋ lee ɛ gyaŋ, na waa mo fa balaŋ baŋ baa yɛɛ ɔ kegyaebii nɛ.

ref: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
text: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ yale mena boe kei dɔɔ na, gyae ke keretii e kpetekpetekpete. Te nyaŋ walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon mɔ boe keŋ de yale n dɔɔ na, nyaŋ ke pɛtɛɛ.”

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: Mena dɔɔ be gyae gbɛɛ keŋ baa mo kyaŋ e, mɔna be yee balaŋ dikpii keŋ. Nawolo nyi bɛɛ lɛɛ di nideli nyi Yesu yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre e.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Keŋte Farasiitena baŋ korowɔ lee yenaŋ gyu ke bake Yesu nyeeman, mena keŋ baa kaa teese ɔ nɔɔman na baa mo ŋon gbagba ɔ nɔɔwoya kyaŋ e.

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: “De Gbeŋgyoo, de naana Mosesi ŋmarase mmaraa kei fa daa nyi, akpaa ŋolo da yem tina ɔ wɛɛle yela te be ta lolo bia ya na, kaboena nyi ɔ naabu waa yala lialo ŋon na baa lola bia ɔ kegyia ŋon yele man.

ref: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
text: Yesu besewɔ kolosi fa balaŋ baŋ aduya man nyiaa,

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba gyoo ŋon ɔ ba do kesaa fa ɔ bu ŋon ɔ ba kpeo alo nɛ.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ tewulɛɛ nyiaa, baa gyu ke baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ nyi baa kɔŋ na be kaa di, mɔna be bɛe ba te kɔŋ ya.

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: Mɔna balaŋ baŋ ɔ be lɔ yele baake nɛ ta mo ɔ nombia ŋan yɛɛ kolo ya. Kei da koro na ɔ te laŋ ɔ wɔɔ, kei mɔ da koro na ɔ te laŋ ɔ tom man.

ref: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
text: Balaŋ akaŋ baŋ kyaŋa gyoo ŋon tewulɛɛ baŋ, te be tuku wɔ te ba koe wɔ.

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: Mena dɔɔ gyoo ŋon gyeŋa baŋ nideli te ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena te be ka koe balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koe ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ nɛ, te ba too be donɔɔ keŋ mɔ.

ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: Keŋte gyoo ŋon baake ɔ tewulɛɛ baŋ bɔɔ kaa nɛ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ‘Ba te saa ma alowɔle weenɛɛ ŋan taŋ, mɔna balaŋ baŋ mɔɔ lɔ yele baake wɔ nɛ, ba tekaboena nyi baa kɔŋ kaa di ya.

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Mena dɔɔ ɛ ŋere gyoo donɔɔ keŋ abɔnterese man, na ɛ ke baake walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɛ kena nɛ, nyi waa kɔŋ alowɔle keŋ tɛɛ.’

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: Mɔna gyoo ŋon ke kɔŋ kaa gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man ɔ kaa kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ pou bɔɔ baake nɛ, ɔ naa baale ŋolo kyaa be man keŋ ɔ ba doona alowɔletɛɛ kegba keŋ kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ waa do nɛ ya.

ref: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
text: Mena dɔɔ yako daa nya agyueŋ. Dei nyi dɛɛ tɔ lempoo fa Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon, yaa de na tɔɔ ya?”

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Mena dɔɔ be kpila be kaseela baŋ man baale kpu na gyoo Herode balaŋ gyu Yesu gyaŋ. Keŋte be yako e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, da gyeŋ nyi n nombia doo na ŋe gbɛɛ, te n na wola balaŋ Wurubuarɛ gbɛɛneŋ kpaakpaa ŋan bo na anokoare. Da gyeŋ nyi ŋolo be tale beo neŋ ya, nawolo nyi n bɛɛ kɛɛ balaŋ siaman ya.

ref: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
text: Ɛ mo kɔbabii keŋ ɛ na mo tɔ lempoo nɛ kɔŋ maa kɛɛ.” Keŋte ba moo kɔbabii keŋ daale kɔna e.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Botɔɔ te be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Romantena gyoo dinaa ŋon.” Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Mena na kpene keŋ yɛɛ Gyoo dinaa ŋon wui na ɛ mo fa e, te kpene keŋ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ mɔ wui na ɛ mo fa e.”

ref: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
text: Be ke nyii Yesu nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nɛ, doo be nɔɔ. Mena dɔɔ be laŋawɔ tina e yela. Yesu kawola lee yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ wose man Maake

ref: At last the woman died.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, te alo ŋon mɔ yeŋawɔ.

ref: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
text: Mosesi mmaraase ŋan pou na Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ pou nombia ŋan bɔɔ wolo nɛ, seŋae mmaraase ala kɛŋa dɔɔ.” Amɔte yɛna nyeelɛɛre Kristo ŋon? Maake

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Mena wee kenaŋ ke nɛ, Sadukiitena baŋ bɛɛ yako nyi yeŋ wɔle kefoŋ be kyaa ya nɛ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ kaa bɔɔse e nyiaa,

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ ta yoo, nawolo nyi ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan ya, te ɛ ba gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ doŋ mɔ ya.

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
text: Ɛ te kala ta Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see ŋan, na ɛ kena kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ be yako lee kefoŋ lee yeŋ man wose man nombia yee? Ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa,

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
text: ‘Maŋ yɛna Aberaham na Asiki na Gyekɔpo Wurubuarɛ.’ Wurubuarɛ te yɛɛ woya Wurubuarɛ e ya, mɔna ɔ yɛɛ balaŋ baŋ be kyaa na be sia nɛ Wurubuarɛ e.”

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ kawola keŋ doo be nɔɔ. Mmaraa dinaate Maake

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
text: Farasiitena baŋ ke nyii nyi Yesu ta mo ɔ nyansa di Sadukiitena baŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be popou kaa gyaŋeewɔ debodokoloŋ.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: “De Gbeŋgyoo, Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan pou na, woŋti kela la?”

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Kei yɛna mmaraa siamante na keŋ ka kela ŋe popou.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Alate keŋ mɔ yɛɛ ŋgba gyaŋgbate keŋ nɛ. Keŋ yɛna nyi, ‘Gyae n dɔɔ ŋgba nyaŋ gbagba n wose nɛ.’

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Farasiitena baŋ ke yilaa debodokoloŋ nɛ, Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa,

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: ‘De Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ŋon yako me Gbeŋgyoo nyi, tisi kyaa me dunoluŋ dɔɔ, kelii debaŋ keŋ maa mo n kɔlala baŋ do n tɛɛ.’

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Sena te ɛ na gyueŋ lee nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ wose man? Ɔ yɛɛ amɔte ɔ naanabii e?” Keŋte be tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ yɛɛ gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii e.”

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Akpaa nyi gyoo Defidi baake nyeelɛɛre ŋon nyi ɔ ‘Gbeŋgyoo’ na, kenaŋ na sena te nyeelɛɛre ŋon waa bese gyoo Defidi ɔ naanabii?”

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Be man ŋolo te tale tiranɔɔ fa Yesu ya. Te lee mena wee kenaŋ ne gyu mɔ na, ŋolo te tale gyɔ ɔ konɔɔ bɔɔse e nombii daale bela ya.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ gyina ɔ wose adido na, Wurubuarɛ waa yɔkɔse e, te ŋon ɔ yɔkɔse ɔ wose na, Wurubuarɛ waa gyina adido.

ref: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
text: Walaŋ ŋon ɔ yɛɛ kegyia e ɛ man nɛ, waa bese ɛ kegbetibii.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: O Gyerusalɛmtena! O Gyerusalɛmtena! Ɛmɛɛ koona Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra, te ɛ fuŋii balaŋ baŋ Wurubuarɛ be kpilii ɛ gyaŋ nɛ boya. Ma te yɛɛ kakyeŋ nyi maa yilaa ŋon do me tɛɛ, ŋgba mena keŋ kegyim talabo ne fɛlɛɛ ɔ beŋkelɛɛ wuu ɔ bia dɔɔ nɛ, mɔna ɛ te fa maŋ gbɛɛ ya.

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Keŋte Yesu kolosiwɔ fa balaŋ dikpii keŋ na ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa,

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: “Gyudatena mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ yɛna balaŋ baŋ be dana gbɛɛ nyii baa tale lese Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan man.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Mena dɔɔ kaboena nyi yaa tei wɔ, na ɛ ke sila kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ wola ŋon nɛ. Mɔna ɛ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ ya, nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne di be kawola keŋ dɔɔ ya.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Bɛɛ bake kasolɔse ŋan kela balaŋ nɛ yere wɔ, mɔna baŋ gbagba bɛɛ sɛɛ yɛɛ kolo kyomii kyɔ wɔ na baa tale seele be kasolɔse ŋenaŋ ya.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Kpene kamasɛ keŋ bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ, bɛɛ yɛɛ bo na balaŋ ke nyiŋ naa wɔ. Wonembu tɔne adakaasebia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ te bɛɛ ŋmarase Wurubuarɛ nombia do ŋe man te bɛɛ mo mataa be tirinɔɔse na be baamense dɔɔ nɛ, bee yeli be wui ŋan na keeree. Te ŋmeeseŋ ŋan bɛɛ mo gbili ba kegba wurikyimse ŋan nɔɔsebia te ŋee wuluŋu nɛ, bee yeli be wui ne yɛɛ dɔɔloŋ dɔɔloŋ.

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Bɛɛ gyae akyaese kpaakpaa be dɛɛgyaŋee di gyoŋ, na Gyudatena ɔsom denɛɛ man.

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: Bɛɛ gyae nyi balaŋ ke buŋii do wɔ nɔɔ balaŋ dɛɛgyaŋee man, te bɛɛ gyae mɔ nyi balaŋ ke baake wɔ nyi ‘Wolala.’

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ dɔɔ, ɛ na yele baa baake ŋon nyi ‘Wolala’ ya, nawolo nyi ɛ popou dana wolale dokoloŋ kooŋ bo, te ɛ yɛɛ walaŋ dokoloŋ mɔ biawɔ.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Te ɛ na baake ŋolo mɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi ɛ ‘Kya’ ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana kya dokoloŋ kooŋ bo. Ŋon yɛna ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na beo liala lɛo ba abɔɔ ŋan be dana nɛ. Ɛ ne fane kyare na ɛ ka mo beo balaŋ nyi ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kpaakpaawɔ. Mena dɔɔ Wurubuarɛ waa wɔŋ ɛ deŋela nideli kela walaŋ kamasɛ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛpo dɔɔ, kila tɛɛle dɔɔ pou nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ walaŋ dokoloŋ na ɔ kaa sila Wurubuarɛ. Mɔna akpaa ɛ nyiŋ walaŋ ŋonaŋ na, ɛ ne yeli ɔ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ koraŋ keŋ nyi diyem keŋ waa naa boalaŋ man nɛ, kela ɛmɛɛ gbagba wui betabola.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ ɛ na wola balaŋ gbɛɛ nɛ, ɛ laako nawolo nyi ɛ kpa akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ntam na ka te yɛɛ kolo ya, mɔna akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ kɔba yayam abɔɔ ŋan doo Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ntam na, kaboena nyi waa di ntam kenaŋ dɔɔ.

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena ayiŋtena, woŋti kela na ke dɔɔ? Kɔba yayam abɔɔ ŋan yaa Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ne yele kɔba abɔɔ ŋan na bese Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Ɛ na bese yako nyi, akpaa ŋolo de kaŋ Wurubuarɛ kedeesa boe keŋ ntam na te yɛɛ kolo ya, mɔna akpaa ɔ kaŋ abɔɔ ŋan gyakaa kedeesa boe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ ntam na, kaboena nyi waa di ntam keŋ dɔɔ.

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena, woŋti kela na ke dɔɔ? Abɔɔ ŋan gyakaa kedeesa boe keŋ dɔɔ nɛ, yaa kedeesa boe keŋ ne yele kedeesa abɔɔ ŋan na bese Wurubuarɛ wui nɛ?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaŋ Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ ntam na, nawolo baa nyi ɔ te kaŋ keŋ na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa ke man nɛ ntam.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Mena mɔ te walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaŋ adido ntam na, nawolo baa nyi ɔ te kaŋ Wurubuarɛ gyoorogbeli keŋ, na Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa ke dɔɔ nɛ ntam.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu gba kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ fa Wurubuarɛ, mɔna ɛ te tina kpene keŋ ne hia nyi yaa yɛɛ mmaraa keŋ man nɛ yela. Ɛ bee di nombia na ŋe gbɛɛ ya, te ɛ bee wii balaŋ waraŋase ya, te ɛ bee di anokoare mɔ ya. Nafɔ kaboena nyi yaa yɛɛ mena abɔɔ kɛŋa pou, na ɛ ka bese fa ɛ wɔɔman abɔɔ buruburu ŋan kufu man dekpeŋ dokoloŋ bela kpu.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Ɛmɛɛ siayɛlɛɛsatena baŋ ɛ dɔŋ balaŋ siaman. Ɛ ne tale kyɔsee lese wonembia buruburu lee ɛ soloŋse man, mɔna ɛ ne lilii wonembia dinaana.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ wɔle bo, mɔna ŋe man dana ayimɛɛ. Nawolo nyi abɔɔ ŋan doo ŋe man nɛ, anibiri na doŋ te ɛ na mo ŋere balaŋ lɛo ŋa.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena siayɛlɛɛsatena, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ ŋan tɔɔman na, ŋe wɔle mɔ gyae ka lee.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako! Nawolo nyi ɛ yɛɛ ŋgba yebɔɔse ŋan bɔɔ mo kolo wolom gyilaa ŋe wɔle na ke yɛɛ kyeo nɛ. Mɔna ŋe tɔɔman na, denibalaŋ ŋɔɔ na ba wosenaane adalɛɛ te wulu ŋe man.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ ne ma Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra na balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ yebɔɔse nideli, na ŋa ke yɛɛ kyeo.

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: Te ɛ ne yako nyi, ‘Daa deŋ kyaa la de naanaɔ debaŋ keŋ nɛ, nafɔ deŋ te kpuu na wɔ na baa koe Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ ya.’

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Mena dɔɔ ɛ nɔɔwoya kɛŋa nawola la nyi, ɛ te sɛɛ nyi balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ koe Wurubuarɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baŋ nɛ, ɛ yɛɛ be duuluŋ man balaŋwɔ.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: Ɛ dana nombiakumɛɛ ŋgba doma nɛ. Ɛ be gyae ɛ ke tale yeu lee Wurubuarɛ nyiŋmaa man, keŋ nyi ɔ be bu ŋon fɔɔ na waa yela ɛ ka gyoo boalaŋ man ya.

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, kɛŋa pou deŋele kewɔŋ gyae ke kɔŋ ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ dɔɔ. Mena keŋ Yesu ne gyae Gyerusalɛm

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Yesu ka lee Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man ɔ ne gyu nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ wɔŋa wa agyueŋ kɔŋ denɛɛ ŋan seŋɛɛ botɔɔ nɛ kyeo dɔɔ.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Keŋte ɔ tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ŋgba ɛ ne naa abɔɔ kɛŋa pou. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, baa tekerii ŋe popou lɔ keŋ nyi ɛ be gyae ɛ kena boe na boe gyakaa ke dɔɔ dɔɔ ya.”

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Nawolo nyi balaŋ burum baa kɔŋ ma yele man, na be kaa mo ba wose nyi baŋ yɛna maŋ nyeelɛɛre ŋon, na baa beo balaŋ burum na baa yo gbɛɛ.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Ɛ nyii yoo nombia na, ɛ na yele ɛ wɔe ke lɔ ŋon ya. Nawolo nyi kaboena nyi nombia kɛŋa pou dɛɛkɔŋ, mɔna kɛŋa bɛɛ wolo nyi ɔto keŋ te lii ya.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Keŋte Yesu gyuuwɔ ke kyaa oliifi bula keŋ dɔɔ. Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ korowɔ gyu ɔ gyaŋ te be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo yako daa, debaŋ woŋti te kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ. Woŋ te dɛɛ mo gyeŋ nyi n kabese kɔŋ, na debaŋ ɔtomante keŋ ta benaa?”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Tɛɛlese baa koro seŋ tia dɔŋa, na gyooneŋ mɔ ka koro seŋ tia dɔŋa. Tanam dinaa gyae ke yala, te tɛɛle mɔ gyae kewosi yenaase burum.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Weeya ŋenaŋ man na, fulɛɛtena na talabose baa laako.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Kɛŋa pou yɛɛ yaa ŋgba fulute kadoŋee kewalaŋ nɛ.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: Lee maŋ dɔɔ balaŋ pou baa kɔla ŋon, na baa kyaŋee ŋon na baa naase ŋon diyem na baa koe ŋon.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Debaŋ kenaŋ man te balaŋ burum baa tina maŋ kelɛɛdi yela. Baa kɔla dɔŋa, na baa lese dɔŋa fa.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Akɛrɛɛ akpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔra baa kɔŋ, na be kaa beo balaŋ burum.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Debaŋ kenaŋ de lii te ŋolo de yako ŋon nyi ‘Ɛ kɛɛ Kristo ŋon kyaa kɛbo yaa ɔ kyaa yenaŋ daale na, ɛ na lɛɛ ke di ya.’

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Mena dɔɔ ɛ fane fa Wurubuarɛ nyi, ɛ keyeu wee kenaŋ na kɔŋ waaretɛɛ yaa kefɛɛfowee ya.

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: Akpaa ɛ naa mmusuo nombii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man, ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ be tɛɛ ɔ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Daniɛle dɔɔ kolosi see lee ka wose man nɛ, na kaboena nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon waa kala mena nɔɔwoya ŋenaŋ na, waa yɛɛ kakyeŋ na waa nyii ŋe man nideli.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: De kɔŋ mena na, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa Gyudia tɛɛle man nɛ, baa yeu gyu bulase dɔɔ.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Te akpaa ŋolo mɔ kyaa wɔɔman na, ɔ na bese gyu dɛɛ nyi ɔ kaa mo ɔ kegba ya.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ taŋ wula kɔkee kayeyɛɛ weeya kɛŋa. Na waa yɛɛ mena ya nɛ, nafɔ ŋolo na ŋolo be gyae waa ka ya. Mɔna lee ɔ balaŋ baŋ ɔ ba lese see dɔɔ nɛ, te waa kɔkee weeya ŋenaŋ.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Mena mɔ te akpaa ɛ naa mena nombia kɛŋa pou ne kɔŋ na, ɛ gyeŋ nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kekɔŋ ta benaa taŋ.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Mena keŋ be kɔŋ Nowa debaŋ keŋ nɛ, mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ kabese kɔŋ gyae ke yɛɛ.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ŋolo ba gyeŋ wee na debaŋ keŋ kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido, na maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon koraŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Gyisɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waageŋ gyeŋa la.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Weeya ŋenaŋ man pɛte loŋ keŋ ke kɔŋ kaa wɔlɛɛ tɛɛle nɛ, na balaŋ ne dii na bɛɛ nyɔɔ. Baala ne yala ala na bɛɛ mo ala mɔ fa yale, kelii wee keŋ Nowa ba gyoo adakaa dinaa keŋ man nɛ.

text: Botɔɔ te Yesu tuuwɔ gyu siaman kyomii te ɔ tisiwɔ buŋ wuluŋu ɔ nyee te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ nyiaa, “Taata, akpaa nyi gyae ke de na, yela mena kayeyɛɛ kei ke laŋ me dɔɔ. Mɔna na maŋ kegyaebii ya. Yɛɛ kpene keŋ nyaŋ gbagba ne gyae.”

ref: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
text: Ɛ koro dee gyu, ɛ kɛɛ walaŋ ŋon waa lese maŋ fa nɛ te tuŋ daa taŋ.” Ba kekyaŋ Yesu Maake

ref: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
text: Keŋte ɔ moo Pita na Sebedeo bia bala baŋ kpu na ɔ wose. Botɔɔ te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa e keŋ nyi ɔ bɛɛ gyeŋ mena keŋ waa yɛɛ ɔ wose ya.

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: Yesu ka bese kɔŋ ɔ kaseela baŋ gyaŋ nɛ, ɔ kaa naawɔ nyi ba te doo dosoroŋ. Keŋte ɔ bɔɔse Pita nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ ɛ te tale deke debaŋ dokoloŋ kooŋ keŋ nyi ɛ be doo yee?”

ref: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
text: Keŋte ɔ besewɔ lee be gyaŋ gyu ɔ kaa fane botoorote. Te ɔ fanewɔ yako nombii dokoloŋ kenaŋ ke bela.

ref: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Me gyoo, yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɔɔ n be kɔŋ kɛbo nɛ.” Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ kaa tɔɔ Yesu do te be kyaŋa e.

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: Yesu ke gyakaa ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ maŋ ŋolo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyudase nɛ kaa leewɔ botɔɔ. Ŋon na balaŋ dikpii keŋ bɔɔ lee saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise gyaŋ nɛ kɔŋala. Na be man baale dana yoosilase na akpokpose.

ref: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
text: Mena dɔɔ Gyudase tɛɛwɔ teeŋ gyu Yesu gyaŋ te ɔ baake e nyi “De Gbeŋgyoo, te ɔ pukaa e doo e nɔɔ.”

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu kaseela baŋ maŋ ŋolo lese ɔ yoosila, te ɔ moowɔ kara saese pou kegyia ŋon tobaale deŋele lese ke lɔ.

ref: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseele ŋon nyiaa, “Mo n yoosila keŋ do ke bɔɔlee man. Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne wɔŋ yoosila na, waa yeŋ bo yoosila nɔɔman.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Saese kegyia ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ kyaŋa ɔ kegba tirii te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɔ te kolosi mmusuo nombia! Woŋ nombii yaa dɛɛ gyae dɛɛ tekaa nyii kpu bela? Nɛnɛɛ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nyii mmusuo nombia ŋan ɔ be kolosi nɛ.

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Mɔna Yesu te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya. Keŋte saese kegyia ŋon yako Yesu nyiaa, “Ma te kɛɛ Wurubuarɛ ŋon ɔ kyaa kekpaakekpaa nɛ nae fa neŋ, akpaa nyaŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ, ŋon ɔ yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ bu dokoloŋ kooŋ ŋon e na, yako daa”

ref: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
text: Te ɔ leewɔ gyu dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ denanɔɔ man. Botɔɔ te alebubii ŋolo mɔ besewɔ naa e te ɔ yako ɔ tebia baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ nyiaa, “Balee kei mɔ kpuɛ na Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Nasarɛte donɔɔ man nɛ.”

ref: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
text: Balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ kyaŋ Yesu nɛ moo e gyu saese pou kegyia Kaifase dekpaŋalaŋ man, botɔɔ keŋ mmaraa wolala na donɔɔ kegyiise pou be gyaŋee nɛ.

ref: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
text: Pita sila Yesu wɔle wɔle menɛɛ kelii saese kegyia ŋon dekpaŋalaŋ man. Ɔ gyoowɔ ke man te ɔ gyuuwɔ ke kyaa tewulɛɛ baŋ bee gyee dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nɛ gyaŋ, na waa naa kpene gyae ke kɔŋ Yesu dɔɔ.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ, ne gyae nombii keŋ baa mo gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ ko e.

ref: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
text: Balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛrɛɛ Yesu nɔɔse, mɔna be te nyiŋ nombiikum keŋ baa mo gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ ya. Wɔle wɔle na te baala bala baale kɔŋawɔ kaa yako nyi,

ref: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Keŋ yaa n yakowɔ nɛ. Mɔna mɛɛ yako ɛ popou ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, wee daale nɛ ɛ kena maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon kyaa Wurubuarɛ doŋ pou gbeŋgyoo ŋon dunoluŋ dɔɔ, na ɛ ka bese naa maŋ na me ta lee ŋɔma man adido mɛɛ kɔŋ.”

ref: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
text: Keŋte be tɔɔ nɔɔloŋse woli ɔ siagbɛɛ te be takee e akɔtase. Baale mɔ ŋmeree e ɔ lakaase man,

text: te be yako e nyiaa “Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Kristo, wola daa ŋon ɔ be gyɔ neŋ.” Pita kebɛɛ Yesu Maake

text: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Pita kyaa dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man. Keŋte saese kegyia ŋon alebu ŋolo kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na Yesu ŋon ɔ ba lee Galelia tɛɛle man nɛ.”

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Te kyare ya nɛ, balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ nɛ kɔŋawɔ Pita gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Kei dɔɔ ke yɛɛ ampaŋ ke kaeŋ nyi nyaŋ mɔ n kpuɛ na balee kei. N kekolosi koraŋ nawolo nyi n yɛɛ be man ŋolo e.”

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Botɔɔ te Pita fiasɛɛ ɔ ne falaa ɔ wose te ɔ kaŋa ntam nyiaa, “Mɔɔ gyeŋ balee kei korakora ya.” Debokenaŋ man te kegyim lɔɔ woya.

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Keŋte Pita tɔɔse nombia ŋan Yesu be yako e nyiaa, “Pɛte kegyim ke lɔ woya na, nyaŋ ke bɛɛ betabotooro nyi n ba gyeŋ maŋ ya” nɛ. Te Pita leewɔ debɔɔman ke kyaŋ ɔ nyee wii.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Nawolo nyi na Pilato gyeŋ nyi, keŋ balaŋ baŋ ne kɔla Yesu dɔɔ te ba lese e fa.

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Be ka lee debɔɔ man bee gyu nɛ, te be gyaŋee na balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene te bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn nɛ. Keŋte ba ŋere e nyi waa lɛɛ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Keŋte kegyiise baŋ moo ba nyeeya baŋ na dɔŋa te be sɛɛwɔ nyi, baa mo kɔba kenaŋ ke lɛɛ tɛɛle saabuse maare ŋolo gyaŋ, na baa kyaa wuu ŋɔɔla botɔɔ.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Mena dɔɔ kaa lii naŋ gyɛŋ nɛ, bɛɛ baake tɛɛle kenaŋ bo nyi fatabo tɛɛle.

ref: So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true, “They took the thirty silver coins, the price of a person among the people of Israel.

ref: They paid it for a potter's field, as the Lord had commanded me.” Pilate Questions Jesus (Mark
text: ke lɛɛ tɛɛle saabuse maare gyaŋ ŋgba mena keŋ de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ ba wola maŋ nɛ.” Be kamo Yesu gyu Pilato gyaŋ Maake

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: Keŋte ba moo Yesu kaa seŋ Pilato ŋon ɔ seŋɛɛ Romantena gyoo dinaa nawɔɔ man nɛ siaman. Te Pilato bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Gyudatena gyoo ŋon?” Te Yesu tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Keŋ yaa n yakowɔ nɛ.”

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Mɔna debaŋ keŋ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise ba mo nombia gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ ya.

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Mɔna Yesu bɛe ɔ te gyuusu ɔ nɔɔ yako nombii dokoloŋ kooŋ gba ya. Nombii kei doo Pilato nɔɔ nideli. Be kalese Yesu fa yeŋ Maake

ref: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
text: Na balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ lam botɔɔ nɛ na tee e na bee kpiisi ba nyeeya,

text: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na kegyiise akaŋ baŋ feefuwɔ do balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyi, baa sola Pilato na waa tina Barabase yela na baa ko Yesu.

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: Pilato kena nyi balaŋ baŋ nɔɔwoya na kela e te ɔ be gyae waa tale yɛɛ kolo lee Yesu wose man ya nɛ, ɔ kyee loŋ faafo ɔ nyiŋmaase balaŋ dikpii keŋ siaman te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Maŋ nyiŋmaa be doo balee kei yeŋ man korakora ya. Ke yɛɛ ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ nombia ŋa.”

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ pou sɛɛwɔ yako nyiaa, “Deŋele kewɔŋ keŋ gyae ke kɔŋ lee ɔ kako wose man nɛ, dɛɛkɔŋ daa na de bia dɔɔ.”

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Keŋte ba moo sɔɔse loo gyooro kotɔtɔɔ wuu e, te ba moo gbutu do e ɔ dunoluŋ nyiŋmaa man na bɛɛ buŋ ɔ siaman kulu e nyiaa, “Gyudatena gyoo nyaŋ na ke yɛɛ. N nyee dɛɛ wolose.”

ref: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
text: Ɔ na mo ɔ wose lɔ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ te ɔ ne yako nyi, ‘Ŋon yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu. Akpaa Wurubuarɛ ne gyae e na, ɛ yela dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi waa lɛɛ e.’ ”

ref: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
text: Be ke kulu e taŋ nɛ, te ba kpee kegba yayam keŋ bɔɔ mo do nɛ te ba moo ŋon gbagba wui abɔɔ do e. Te ba moo lee be kaa gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ. Ba kegyɔ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ Maake

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Botɔɔ te ba moo tee ŋaŋte daale mo kyakale na soloŋ fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ. Mɔna ɔ ke daa ke kɛɛ nɛ te ɔ bɛɛwɔ nyi ɔ be nyɔɔ ya.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ba ke gyɔ e sakaa ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ, te be kpɛlɛɛ ɔ kegbase buŋii te be lɔɔ boe ŋe dɔɔ na baa naa keŋ walaŋ kamasɛ waa nyiŋ.

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, te be kyaawɔ botɔɔ bɛɛ deke e.

ref: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text: Keŋte be ŋmarase nombii keŋ dɔɔ bɔɔ gyɔ e ɔpaŋdaŋ dɔɔ nɛ mataa ɔ nyee gbɛɛ nyiaa, “kei yɛna yesu, gyudatena gyoo ŋon.”

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
text: na bɛɛ yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ ŋon n kpa n ke tekerii Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ na n ka mo weeya atooro ma ke nɛ, lɛɛ n wose na dɛɛ kɛɛ. Akpaa nyaŋ yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon na, lɛɛ n wose na n ke tisi lee ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ dɔɔ.”

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Keŋte balaŋ akaŋ baŋ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ kɛɛ nyi Elaegya waa kɔŋ kaa lɛɛ e?”

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Lee weese kaseŋ dɛɛ kelii ɔ ke gɛɛse nɛ, ditiŋtɛɛ kaa lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Debaŋ kenaŋ nɛ, te Yesu fae yeesa keŋkeŋ nyiaa, “Eli, Eli lama sabatani?” Keŋ nawolo nyi, “Me Wurubuarɛ me Wurubuarɛ, weera te n lese n sia yela maŋ?”

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Debokenaŋ man te be man ŋolo yeuwɔ ka mo kolo ŋgba kokyaa keŋ gyae ke tale lɛɛ loŋ nɛ do soloŋ denyaŋesa daale man, te ɔ moo ke tooso na daŋ te ɔ gyinaa ke adido fa Yesu nyi waa nyɔɔ.

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Kekɔŋ mena nɛ, kanyaŋ keŋ bɔɔ mo kpase Wurubuarɛ ɔsom deni dinaa keŋ man nɛ bɛɛsewɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ ala lee adido kaa lii ka mokɔɔ man. Tɛɛle wosiwɔ, te boya dinaana mɔ yɛlɛɛwɔ.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Yesu kefoŋ lee yeŋ man nɛ, Wurubuarɛ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ foŋii lee ba yebɔɔse man nɛ, gyuu Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man ka lese ba wose wola balaŋ burum.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Romantena yoonɔɔtena siamante ŋon na baŋ ba seŋɛɛ bɛɛ gyee Yesu nɛ kena tɛɛle kewosi na gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan be yɛɛ pou nɛ, ba wose yeŋa wɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balee kei yɛna Wurubuarɛ bu ŋon ampaŋ.”

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Na ala burum baale mɔ seŋɛɛ yenaŋ botɔɔ lemlem bɛɛ kɛɛ. Be wulaa be silana Yesu leenaŋ Galelia donɔɔ man bɛɛ kɛɛ ɔ dɔɔ.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: Ɔ gyuu Pilato gyaŋ ka sola e nyi waa fa e gbɛɛ na waa mo Yesu woo ŋon ke wuu. Keŋte Pilato sɛɛwɔ nyi baa moo Yesu woo ŋon fa e.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: Keŋte Gyosɛfo moo woo ŋon, te ɔ moo akaralɛɛ wɔle wɔle daale milii e.

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: Te ɔ moo e ke wuu boe bɔɔ wɔle keŋ ŋon Gyosɛfo gbagba be kare see ɔ wose nɛ man. Keŋte ɔ biliŋi boe dinaa daale tɔ yebɔɔ keŋ nɔɔ te ɔ laŋawɔ.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Na Mɛɛre ŋon ɔ ba lee Magedala nɛ, na Mɛɛre balate ŋon baŋɛɛ botɔɔ be sia tee na yebɔɔ keŋ.

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: Tɛɛ kena keŋ ke yɛɛ Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee ke nɛ, Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Farasiitena baŋ gyuuwɔ ke gyaŋee Pilato dekpaŋalaŋ man.

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: Keŋte be yako Pilato nyiaa, “Kegyia dɛɛ tɔɔse nyi Yesu dekarate ŋon ke kyaa keŋ nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, weeya atoorote wɔle na waa foŋ lee yeŋ man.

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Mena dɔɔ yela be kaa deke yebɔɔ keŋ na baa gyee ke nideli kelii weeya atoorote keŋ. Na mena ya na ɔ kaseela baŋ baa yo gyu ke ŋmɛɛle e lese na baa yako nyi, ɔ foŋae lee yeŋ man. Na mena ya na, dekara alate kei gyae ka kela gyaŋgba te keŋ.”

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Keŋte Pilato yako wɔ nyiaa, ɛ lese ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ balaŋ na baa gyu ka deke yebɔɔ keŋ nideli fa ŋon ŋgba mena keŋ baa tale nɛ.

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Mena dɔɔ Yesu kaseela kufu daale akaŋ baŋ gyuuwɔ Galelia tɛɛle man, te be gyuu bula keŋ dɔɔ Yesu ba wola wɔ nyi baa gyu nɛ.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: Botɔɔ te Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ gyaŋeewɔ, te be bakewɔ te be faa yoonɔɔtena baŋ bɛɛ deke yebɔɔ keŋ nɛ kɔba burum daale,

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: na ɛ ke ta bileŋbileŋ ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa ‘Ɔ te fom lee woya man te ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e. Kei yɛna nombii keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon.’ ”

text: “Debaŋ keŋ te lii, te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ mɔ ta benaa. Ɛ kyɛɛkee lee ɛ gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ, na ɛ ke lɛɛ nombia kpaakpaa ŋan di!”

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Lewi baake Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyu ɔ dɛɛ be kaa di. Lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra burum sila Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyu botɔɔ, te baŋ na wɔ pou ke diiwɔ bom.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Farasiitena dikpii keŋ man kena ŋgba Yesu ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra nɛ be bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te Yesu ne dii bom na lempoolɛɛra na nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Wee daale balaŋ baale naawɔ na Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be nɔɔ. Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre silala na Farasiitena ne bake be nɔɔ, te nyaŋ wui kɛwɔ dɔɔ bɔɔ ne bake be nɔɔ ya?”

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Mɔna debaŋ daale gyae kekɔŋ keŋ baa mo alowɔle baale ŋon lee be gyaŋ. Mena wee kenaŋ man te baa bake be nɔɔ.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Ŋolo bɛɛ kara kanyaŋ wɔle mo taa kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ ba yem nɛ bɔɔ ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena nɛ, kanyaŋ wɔle keŋ gyae ke tao kakyeŋgbelaa keŋ wolose ke bɔɔ keŋ yɛɛ ke dinaa.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Mena mɔ te ŋolo bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle do wonembu tɔne ogele bene man ya. Akpaa ɔ yɛɛ mena na soloŋ keŋ gyae ke tao tɔne keŋ lii, na soloŋ keŋ na wonembu tɔne ogele keŋ pou ke wɔlɛɛ. Bɛɛ mo soloŋ wɔle bo do wonembu tɔne ogele wɔle man.” Yesu kawola lee kefɛɛfowee wose man Mateo

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
