ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Keŋte Farasiitena bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera te n kaseela baŋ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ da kefɛɛfowee?”

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Mena dɔɔ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon yɛna kefɛɛfowee keŋ gba Gbeŋgyoo.”

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Balaŋ baŋ booroŋ dɔɔ ɔ yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa gyae degbele fa e, na balaŋ baŋ be nyiŋ tɔ e do ya.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Na balaŋ baŋ baale na deke Yesu nyi waa kyɔ balee ŋon kawee ba kefɛɛfowee keŋ, na baa nyiŋ mo nombia gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako baam dayeŋasate ŋon nyiaa, “Lee kaa seŋ siaman kɛbo.”

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: Te Yesu kɛɛ wɔ pou gyaŋee baŋ man, te takaa e nideli lee ba deŋele doŋ keŋ dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ yako kaweete ŋon nyiaa, “Tenee n baam.” Botɔɔ te balee ŋon tenee ɔ baam keŋ, te kawee keŋ taŋawɔ te ɔ baam keŋ yɛɛ doŋ.

ref: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
text: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ wɔŋa ba wose lee botɔɔ gyu Galelia boo nɔɔman, te balaŋ burum baŋ bɔɔ lee Galelia nɛ sila e gyu botɔɔ.

ref: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
text: Baala kufu bala baŋ Yesu ba lese nɛ yela yaa, Simɔn te ɔ doo e yele nyi Pita, na

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Keŋte Yesu gyeŋa bula daale, te ɔ baake balaŋ baŋ ɔ ne gyae nɛ te be kaa yilaawɔ ɔ gyaŋ.

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: na Anderiase na Filipo na Batolomeo na Mateo na Tomase na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Tadeose na Kaanan baale Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ,

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Botɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man kɔŋ botɔɔ nɛ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Feliŋkumɛɛ pou gyoo ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Bɛlisɛbu nɛ doola Yesu man. Ŋon faa na e doŋ te ɔ na mo gberaa feliŋkumɛɛ lee balaŋ man nɛ.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Na balaŋ burum kyaa kilisi e te be yako e nyiaa, “Kɛɛ, n naa na n naabia seŋɛɛ debɔɔman bɛɛ gyae neŋ.”

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Keŋte Yesu baake balaŋ baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ yako wɔ aduya man nyiaa, “Sena te ɔbɔnsam waa tale gegi ɔ wose?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Yesu ka lese ɔ kpilala baŋ taŋ nɛ te ɔ gyuuwɔ ɔ dɛɛ. Keŋte balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ ŋon na ɔ kaseela bee nyiŋ gbɛɛ baa di weenɛɛ paŋpaŋ gba ya.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Akpaa tɛɛle daale man balaŋ de kpase ba wose bee yoo na dɔŋa nɛ, tɛɛle kenaŋ be gyae ke seŋ ya.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ŋolo be tale gyu doŋte ŋolo dɛɛ na ɔ kaa mo ɔ kolo, keŋ nyi ɔ te di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ ya. Nyi akpaa ɔ di doŋte ŋon dɔɔ te ɔ bake e see nɛ, kenaŋ na waa tale kuu wa abɔɔ pou.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Mɛɛ yako ŋon anokoare baa ɛ ke nyii nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ waa tale mo balaŋ pou nombiakumɛɛ na mmusuo nombia ŋan bɛɛ yɛɛ nɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Mɔna walaŋ ŋon waa kolosi mmusuo nombia tia Wurubuarɛ feliŋ ŋon nɛ, ɔ be gyae waa mo kyɛɛ ɔtenate korakora ya. Nawolo nyi walaŋ ŋonaŋ te yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛ ŋan ŋe doo kekpaakekpaa.

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Keŋ dɔɔ te Yesu be yako nombia kɛŋa yɛna nyiaa, na balaŋ ne yako nyi feliŋkum doo la ɔ man. Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia Mateo

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Keŋte Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ debɔɔman kpila nyi baa baake e fa wɔ.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Bomɔ yɛna me naa na me naabia?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Keŋte ɔ kɛɛ baŋ be kyaa kilisi e nɛ gyaŋee te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Kɛɛ, me naa na me naabia yɛna kɛwɔ.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne yɛɛ Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii nɛ, ŋon yɛna me naabu na me deesi na me naa.”

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Wee daale Yesu besewɔ ɔ na wola Wurubuarɛ nombia Galelia boo nɔɔman. Te mena keŋ balaŋ burum be kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man gyu loŋ keŋ dɔɔ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Ɔ kɛɛ ŋmatee ŋa nɛ, ŋaale yalawɔ gbɛɛneŋbia ŋan doo wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ man, te gyebuse kaa tɔɔsee ŋa di.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Botɔɔ te ɔ wola wɔ nombia aduya man.

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ tei kɛbo, wɔɔfarale ŋolo gyuuwɔ ke ŋmatee abɔɔbia ŋgba amɔla nɛ ɔ wɔɔman.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Mena dɔɔ ɔga kaa seŋawɔ na te ŋa botowɔ. Te keŋ ŋe te nyiŋ lila burum dɔɔ ya nɛ, ŋe popou yekeewɔ.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Bia ŋan ŋaale mɔ yalawɔ kpalamse man te ŋe tuuwɔ, te kpalamse ŋan mɔ tuuwɔ milii ŋa. Mena dɔɔ ŋe te nyiŋ ŋa wose se bia ya.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu besewɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Woŋ te dɛɛ mo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ maa na? Yaa dudu woŋti te dɛɛ gyɔ na dɛɛ mo lese ke man?

ref: times as much as was planted. Light (Luke
text: ), nɛ.” Yesu kegyɔ dudu lee kanea wose man Mateo

ref: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
text: Keka Yesu waageŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela kufu bala baŋ na balaŋ baŋ be kyaa botɔɔ kpu na wɔ nɛ yako e nyi waa wola wɔ dudu keŋ asɛɛ.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Mɔna keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan te nyiŋ lila be man dɔɔ ya nɛ, bɔɔ ne tale seŋ keŋkeŋ kelii ɔto ya. Mena dɔɔ akpaa de kɔŋ nyi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan dɔɔ te bɛɛ naase wɔ diyem na, be ta bese be wɔle bileŋ.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: na ke yela, ‘Baa kɛo kɛo mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya, te baa tei tei mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Na mena ya nɛ, baa kyɛɛkee lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man, na Wurubuarɛ ka mo be nombiakumɛɛ kyɛɛ wɔ.’ ” Yesu kalese dudu keŋ asɛɛ Mateo

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Abɔɔbia ŋan wɔɔfarale ŋon be ŋmatee nɛ seŋɛɛ fa Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ŋolo bɛɛ kyaŋee kanea na waa mo kolo wuu ke dɔɔ yaa waa mo ke do mpa ateta ya. Mɔna ɔ na mo ke bo gyakaa kolo dɔɔ adido.

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba mena keŋ walaŋ ne ŋmatee abɔɔbia ŋaale ŋgba amɔla ɔ wɔɔman nɛ.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Tɛɛle keŋ gbagba ne yele la te bia ŋan ne tuu. Ne taŋgbɛɛ yɛɛ faareŋse na te fulu, pɛte kaa yɛɛ bia.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Amɔla bia ŋan de yɛɛ pɛ, na wɔɔte ŋon ta mo ɔ sakawa gyu ke kara ŋa, nawolo nyi amɔla ŋan kekara debaŋ te lii.” Daŋbii keŋ bɛɛ baake ke nyi Mastade nɛ dudu Mateo

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ŋgba daŋbii peperee daale keŋ bɛɛ baake nyi Mastade nɛ. Wɔɔ man abɔɔ ŋan bee duu pou nɛ, daŋbii kei yɛna ŋe man kemaasa.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Mɔna nyi akpaa be duu ke nɛ, kɛɛ bee bese kolo dinaa kela daasebia akaŋ ŋan pou, keŋ nyi gyebuse ne kɔŋ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.”

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Ɔ te kolosi nombii yakaa fa wɔ keŋ ɔ te gyɔ dudu ya. Mɔ nyi akpaa de ka ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ baageŋ nɛ, ɔ na lese aduya ŋan pou man wola wɔ. Yesu keyɔkɔse feliŋ ɛpo dɔɔ Mateo

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Mena wee kenaŋ balinɔɔ nɛ, Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ toŋ gyu boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.”

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Aduya burum ŋgba kei dinɔɔ nɛ te Yesu moowɔ kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa balaŋ baŋ, ŋgba mena keŋ baa tale nyii ŋe man nideli nɛ.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Keŋte be tinaa balaŋ dikpii keŋ yela, te ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋawɔ. Na agbelese ŋaale mɔ silana wɔ.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Be kee gyu na te feliŋ dinaa daale korowɔ ne gyɔɔ, te gyɔɔ loŋ do degbele keŋ man te ke yela ke munu.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Debaŋ kenaŋ na Yesu te doo dosoroŋ degbele keŋ wɔle mo ɔ nyee gyakaa keputu dɔɔ. Keŋte ɔ kaseela baŋ ke gyuusu e bɔɔse e nyiaa, “De Gbeŋgyoo, n be famenɛ ŋgba dɛɛ gyae dee munu yee?”

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ te ɔ fae feliŋ keŋ te ɔ yako loŋ keŋ nyiaa, “Yɛɛ yididi!” Keŋte feliŋ keŋ tinaa kegyɔ yela, te loŋ keŋ mɔ yɛɛwɔ yididi.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa, “Woŋ dɔɔ te ɛ dana gyakoloŋ mena? Ɛ sɛɛ ɛ te lɛɛ ta maŋ di yee?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Gyakoloŋ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ te be bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, “Walaŋ woŋti yaa kei, te feliŋ na loŋ gba ne nyii ɔ dei nɛ?”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako e nyiaa, “Me bu, n kelɛɛ maŋ di dɔɔ te n nyiŋa kawee kekyɔ. Ta wosefɛɛreŋ man, nawolo nyi n kaale keŋ pou te taŋ.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Ɔ te naa diyem nideli teeneŋtena nyiŋmaa man, te ɔ wɔlɛɛ ɔ kɔba keŋ pou ɔ dana nɛ nyi waa nyiŋ kawee kekyɔ. Mɔna ɔ kawee keŋ na bese bo yɛɛ doŋ kpu.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Yesu ka gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse balaŋ baŋ nyiaa, “Weera te ɛ ne wii te ɛ ne fae yeesa? Bu ŋon ta yeŋa ta ya, ɔ doo dosoroŋ bo.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ toŋa boo keŋ gyu Gerasenetena tɛɛle man.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Na mena balee ŋonaŋ kyae amusee man botɔɔ, te ŋolo na ŋolo bɛɛ tekaa tale kyaŋ e do agbaraagbaraa bela ya.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Nawolo nyi nafɔ bɛɛ mo agbaraagbaraa do e ɔ nyiŋmaase na ɔ nawɔɔ man, mɔna ɔ ne tirii ŋa pou leki. Te ŋolo be dana doŋ keŋ waa tale yɔkɔse e ya.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Nelim na ka weese na ɔ dɔŋ amusee man na bulase dɔɔ botɔɔ ɔ ne fae yeesa, na ɔ na mo boya kɛrɛɛ ɔ wose.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Balee ŋonaŋ kena Yesu ne kɔŋ lemlem nɛ, te ɔ yeuwɔ ke buŋ ɔ siaman.

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Keŋte balee ŋon waase Yesu agbaatee agbaatee nyi, ɔ na gegi feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan lee depuŋ kenaŋ man ya.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Kekɔŋ mena na kekuiriise dikpii daale seŋɛɛ bula keŋ teŋa man bee dii.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Keŋte feliŋkumɛɛ ŋan doo Yesu teree nyi, “Waaseda, fa daa gbɛɛ na de kaa gyoo kekuiriise kɛwɔ man.”

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu sɛɛwɔ te ba leewɔ ka gyoo kekuiriise baŋ man. Kekuiriise baŋ baa yɛɛ ŋgba kakpoŋse ala nɛ, te be yeuwɔ tisi dekeŋkeŋ kyoloŋkyoloŋ keŋ gyu ka gyoo loŋ man, te be popou munuwɔ.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ kɛɛ kekuiriise baŋ dɔɔ nɛ yeuwɔ, te be gyuuwɔ ke yako kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ fa balaŋ baŋ be kyaa donɔɔ keŋ man nɛ na akuraase ŋan man pou. Keŋte balaŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa kɛɛ kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Mena dɔɔ te kyare ya te balaŋ kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ te be naa walaŋ ŋon nafɔ feliŋkumɛɛ doo ɔ man nɛ baŋɛɛ, na ɔ doona wa abɔɔ na ɔ diteŋ keŋ te taŋ. Ba kena nyi balee ŋon diteŋ te taŋ nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Keŋte balaŋ baŋ waase Yesu nyi waa lee be tɛɛle dɔɔ.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Mɔna Yesu te sɛɛ ya te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ta gyu n dɛɛ, na n kaa yako n loŋtona kpene keŋ n Gbeŋgyoo be yɛɛ fa neŋ, na mena keŋ ɔ be wii n waraŋase nɛ.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Mena dɔɔ balee ŋon laŋawɔ, te ɔ gyuuwɔ donɔɔse kufu ŋan ŋe kyaa dupuŋ kenaŋ man nɛ ɔ ne yako kpene keŋ Yesu be yɛɛ fa e nɛ. Te gyakoloŋ gyoo balaŋ baŋ pou. Yesu kegyuusu Gyariose bu alebubii ŋolo, na ɔ kekyɔ alo ŋon fatabo ne gyɔ e nɛ kawee Mateo

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Botɔɔ te balaŋ baŋ boŋo Yesu. Keŋte ɔ gegi balaŋ baŋ pou lee te ɔ moo bu ŋon ɔ kya na ɔ naa, na ɔ kaseela batooro baŋ be silana e nɛ, te be gyuuwɔ deni keŋ man woo ŋon doo nɛ.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu besewɔ gyoo degbele man toŋ boo keŋ gyu diŋgyiŋ. Keŋte balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ boo nɔɔman botɔɔ.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Keŋte Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia ŋon bɛɛ baake e nyi Gyariose nɛ kɔŋawɔ botɔɔ. Ɔ kena Yesu nɛ, ɔ ke buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ,

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: te ɔ waase e nideli nyiaa, “Me bu alebubii ne gyae waa yeŋ. Kɔŋ na n kaa mo n nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ na waa nyiŋ nyeedoŋ.”

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Mena dɔɔ Yesu sila e laŋ. Be kee gyu na balaŋ burum silana wɔ, na bɛɛ soko e.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Na alo ŋolo kyaa keŋ fatabo ne gyɔ e kulutooneŋ kufu ala yaa.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Karatetee dokoloŋ te fatabo keŋ ne gyɔ e nɛ karawɔ, te ɔ naawɔ ɔ wose man nyi ɔ kawee te taŋ.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu naawɔ nyi doŋ daale ta lee ɔ man. Keŋte ɔ besenawɔ kɛɛ balaŋ baŋ gyaŋee te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Amɔte tinaa na ma kegba?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Botɔɔ te ɔ kaseela baŋ yako e nyiaa, “Nyaŋ gbagba n naa mena keŋ balaŋ be wuu n dɔɔ, na n na bese bɔɔse nyi ‘Amɔte tinaa na maŋ?’ ”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Mɔna Yesu gyakaa ɔ ne kɛo nyi waa naa walaŋ ŋon ɔ be tina ɔ kegba nɛ.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Alo ŋon kena mena keŋ ɔ wose be yɛɛ e nɛ, ɔ moo gyakoloŋ na wose keseree kaa buŋ Yesu nawɔɔtɛɛ te ɔ yako e kpene keŋ be kɔŋ nɛ pou.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Yesu ka seŋ botɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, baale leewɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ kaa yako e nyiaa, “N bu alebu ŋon ta yem. Weera te n ne naase de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon diyem bela”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Mena dɔɔ Yesu te yela ŋolo ke sila e kpu na Pita na Gyemisi, na Gyemisi ɔ naabu Gyɔn baageŋ ya.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Be ka benaa Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ nɛ, Yesu nyiiwɔ na balaŋ ne wii na bɛɛ fae yeesa keŋkeŋ.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Karatetee dokoloŋ te alebu ŋon korowɔ seŋ ɔ ne tɛɛ. Keŋte gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli. Na bu ŋon te di kulutooneŋ kufu ala.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nideli nyi be na yeli walaŋ na walaŋ ke nyii nombii kei ya. Te ɔ yako wɔ nyi baa gyae kolo fa bu ŋon ke di.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Keŋte ba toŋa boo keŋ gyu Genesareti tɛɛle man, te be kpaŋa ba degbele keŋ botɔɔ.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ ne bu Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre yenaŋ kamasɛ, mɔna ŋon gbagba ɔ donɔɔ man balaŋ, na ɔ loŋtona na ɔ dekpaŋalaŋmantena bee buu na e ya.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Gyoo Herode kenyii mena nɛ ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Gyɔn ŋon mɔɔ kara ɔ nyee nɛ bese la foŋ nɛ!”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Yesu kpilala kufu bala baŋ ɔ be kpilii nɛ besewɔ kɔŋ kaa yako e nombia ŋan pou bɔɔ yɛɛ, na ŋan pou bɔɔ wola balaŋ nɛ.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Gyudatena kefɛɛfowee kelii nɛ, ɔ korowɔ gyu bɔ ɔsom deni man ɔ na wola wɔ Wurubuarɛ nombia. Ɔ nombia ŋan ɔ na wolo nɛ doo balaŋ baŋ pou nɔɔ te be bɔɔsewɔ nyiaa, “Aleŋ yaa balee kei nyiŋa abɔɔ kɛŋa pou lee? Aleŋ yaa ɔ nyiŋa mena nyansa kei lee? Kɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia ŋan ɔ ne yɛɛ?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Na kapenta ŋon yaa yee? Na ɔ naa yɛna Mɛɛre yee? Na ɔ naabia yɛna Gyemisi na Gyosɛfo na Simɔn na Gyuda yee? Na daa na ɔ desina kyaa la kɛbo yee?” Mena dɔɔ ba te lɛɛ e do ya.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Keŋte ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ na mo kolo tela kpu na ɛ gbutuse ya. Ɛ na mo weenɛɛ, yaa akpo yaa kɔba tela ya.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Te akpaa ɛ gyu yenaŋ daale te ba te lɛɛ ŋon yaa be te nyii ŋon ya nɛ, ɛ lee botɔɔ na ɛ kpiisi ya nkyokotase aŋasa lɔ wɔ na ke di adansɛɛ tia wɔ nyi Wurubuarɛ be gyae waa tina wɔ yela ya.”

ref: Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!” So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
text: Nawolo nyi na Gyɔn gyakaa ɔ ne yako gyoo Herode nyi, “Gbɛɛ be doo nyi waa mo ɔ kegyia Filipo wɛɛle yala ŋgba mena keŋ ɔ be yɛɛ nɛ ya.” Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode gbagba yela be kyaŋa Gyɔn te ba doo e agbaraagbaraase mo e ketɔ deni.

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Nombii kei dɔɔ Herodia moo Gyɔn do ɔ tɔɔ man, te ɔ gyae gbɛɛ keŋ waa mo ko e mɔna ɔ bɛɛ tale ya.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Nawolo nyi gyoo Herode gyeŋ nyi Gyɔn yɛɛ walaŋ ŋon ɔ gbɛɛneŋ tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman, te ɔ ta mo ɔ wose mɔ fa Wurubuarɛ. Mena dɔɔ gyoo Herode na weese Gyɔn keŋ nyi nombiikum daale be yɛɛ e ya. Debaŋ kamasɛ keŋ gyoo Herode de nyii Gyɔn nɔɔwoya na bɛɛ seŋ e nideli ya, mɔna ɔ ne gyae nyi waa tei e.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Botɔɔ te alebu ŋon leewɔ yenaŋ gyu ke bɔɔse ɔ naa nyiaa, “Nna, woŋ te maa bɔɔse?” Botɔɔ te ɔ naa yako e nyiaa, “Yako nyi n ne gyae Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre ŋon nyee.”

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Debokenaŋ man te alebu ŋon barasewɔ gyu gyoo Herode gyaŋ ke yako e nyiaa, “Mɛɛ gyae n ke kara Gyɔn Wurubuarɛ loŋsɔɔre nyee do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man fa maŋ.”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Nombii kei takaa gyoo Herode nideli, mɔna ntam keŋ ɔ be kam ŋɔɔla baŋ siaman dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ te tale bɛɛ ya.

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Keŋte ɔ moo ke do tasa taŋtaŋalaŋ man kɔna gyoo Herode te ɔ moowɔ fa alebu ŋon, te ŋon mɔ moo ke fa ɔ naa.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Gyɔn kaseela baŋ kenyii ɔ yeŋ nɛ, be gyuuwɔ ka mo e ke wuu. Yesu kefa balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ weenɛɛ Mateo

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Na mena keŋ balaŋ na gyem tisi be dɔɔ beiŋ dɔɔ nɛ, be te nyiŋ gbɛɛ baa di weenɛɛ gba ya. Mena dɔɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ yela dɛɛ wɔŋ da wose lee yenaŋ gyu botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be kyaa ya nɛ, na ɛ kaa fɛɛfo.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Keŋte ba gyoowɔ degbele man te be laŋa botɔɔ keŋ balaŋ be kyaa ya nɛ.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Mena debaŋ kenaŋ na te lii balinɔɔ, mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ ɔ gyaŋ kaa yako e nyiaa, “Kɛbo yɛɛ ŋale nsana ke, te tɛɛ mɔ te yuu.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Mɔ Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyae kolo fa wɔ na baa di.” Keŋte be bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Da mo tomyɛɛre kulutoo dokoloŋ kɔba lɛɛ weenɛɛ koraŋ na, gyae ka yee balaŋ burum kɛwɔ pou?”

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu mɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Bodobodose aŋmoŋ te ɛ dana? Ɛ gyu ke kɛɛ.” Be ke gyu ke kɛɛ nɛ te be kaa yako e nyiaa, “Bodobodose abiliŋ anoŋ na kpebia bala pɛ te de dana.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa yela balaŋ baŋ ke kyakee akpii akpii ŋalese nalaŋ ŋan dɔɔ.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Nawolo nyi be popou naa e te gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ. Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kolosiwɔ na wɔ nyi, “Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ! Ɛ na yee ya! Maŋ e.”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu moo bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala baŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee kɛɛ adido te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ. Te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Balaŋ baŋ pou diiwɔ baa ba kegyaebii.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Be ke di taŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ tɔɔsee bodobodose na kpebia buruburu ŋan bɔɔ dii kaase na, be nyiŋa doŋase kufu ala (

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Debokenaŋ man te Yesu yela ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man kyaŋ gbɛɛ bee gyu Bɛtesaida donɔɔ keŋ ke kyaa boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ nɛ, na ŋon ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Ɔ ke lɔ balaŋ baŋ gbɛɛ taŋ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ bula dɔɔ ɔ kaa fane.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Kelii balinɔɔ nɛ, na degbele keŋ te lii boo keŋ nsana nsana, te kaa Yesu waageŋ boo keŋ diŋgyiŋ.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu kɛɛwɔ na feliŋ ne gyɔɔ gyaŋee na ɔ kaseela baŋ, mena dɔɔ bɛɛ kaa na degbele keŋ kesaa. Keŋte ɔ karatɛɛwɔ ta loŋ keŋ dɔɔ gyu be gyaŋ, te ɔ yɛɛwɔ ŋgba ɔ ne gbaa wɔ nɛ.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Ɔ kaseela baŋ kena e ɔ dɔŋ loŋ keŋ dɔɔ ɔ ne kɔŋ nɛ, na ba gyeŋ nyi ɔ yɛɛ woo e te be fae yeesa.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Botɔɔ te ɔ gyoowɔ kyaa degbele keŋ man kpu na wɔ, te feliŋ ŋon nɔɔ yɔkɔsewɔ. Nombia kɛŋa doo ɔ kaseela baŋ nɔɔ nideli.

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: Yesu ka mo bodobodose anoŋ kpala balaŋ kakpoŋse banoŋ baŋ nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ te nyii ka asɛɛ ya, nawolo nyi na be sɛɛ be ta gyeŋa walaŋ ŋon dinɔɔ Yesu yɛɛ ya. Yesu kekyɔ kaweese Genesareti tɛɛle man Mateo

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Be ka betaa lee degbele keŋ man pɛ, te balaŋ naa Yesu te ba gyeŋawɔ nyi ŋon ŋonaŋ.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ yekiiwɔ lee tɛɛle kenaŋ yenaŋ kamasɛ man kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ be nyii nyi Yesu kyaa na, bɛɛ kɔŋ na ba seele be kaweesetena ba afoŋoŋse dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ botɔɔ.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Te akuraase yaa donɔɔse kamasɛ ŋan man Yesu de gyu nɛ, balaŋ na mo be kaweesetena kaa see abɔntere man, na ba te waase e nyi waa yela kaweesetena baŋ ka mo be nyiŋmaase tina ɔ kegba nɔɔbii paŋpaŋ bo gba. Te baŋ pou bɔɔ tina ɔ kegba keŋ nɛ, wose yɛɛ wɔ doŋ.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Wee daale nɛ, Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baale baŋ bɔɔ lee Gyerusalɛm donɔɔ man nɛ, kaa yilaawɔ Yesu gyaŋ.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ gyeŋ adao gbɛɛneŋ ŋan ɛ na mo bɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, na ɛ ke nyiŋ di ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan dɔɔ.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Nawolo nyi Farasiitena na Gyudatena baŋ pou bee tina ba beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan yela ya. Mena dɔɔ nyi akpaa ba te faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ be naanaɔ be yako see ya na bɔɔ ne dii ya.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Mena ke mɔ te akpaa ba lee kebu man kɔŋ na, bɛɛ saase ba wose pɛte baa di weenɛɛ. Ba beneŋ amanberɛse boe nideli. Be dana gbɛɛneŋ ŋan bɛɛ mo saase ba leenɔɔse na tasase na osuŋse mɔ.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Mena dɔɔ Farasiitena na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te n kaseela baŋ bee di da beneŋ amanberɛse dɔɔ ya? Nawolo nyi bɔɔ ne faafo be nyiŋmaase ŋgba mena keŋ da beneŋ amanberɛ nawolo nɛ pɛ baa di ya!”

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: Bɛɛ som maŋ bo yakaa, nawolo nyi mmaraase ŋan denibalaŋ be yɛɛ nɛ te be dana bɛɛ wola balaŋ nombia.’

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Ɛ te tina Wurubuarɛ mmaraase ŋan yela, te ɛ dana ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan balaŋ na wolo nɛ man.”

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Mɔna ɛmɛɛ ne yako nyiaa, ‘Akpaa ŋolo de yako ɔ kya yaa ɔ naa nyi, “Kpene keŋ nafɔ me dana maa mo kyɔ neŋ nɛ yɛɛ Koriban ke.” ’ .

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Mena yaa ɛ na mo ɛ beneŋ amanberɛse ŋan wɔlɛɛ Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan, te ɛ yɛɛ ŋa see ɛ bia mɔ. Te ɛ ne yɛɛ nombia burum ŋan ŋe yɛɛ ŋgba kɛŋa dinɔɔ nɛ kpu.” Kpene keŋ ne yɛɛ walaŋ ayimɛɛ Mateo

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Kpene kamasɛ keŋ na lee walaŋ konɔɔ man nɛ, keŋ ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle nɛ, Yesu besewɔ baake balaŋ baŋ pou kaa yilaa ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛ popou ɛ tei maŋ, na ɛ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa man nideli.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Botɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Mena dɔɔ ɛmɛɛ mɔ bee nyii nombia asɛɛ yee? Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi kpene kamasɛ keŋ na lee yenaŋ gyoo walaŋ wose man na, ke bɛɛ yɛɛ e ayimɛɛ yee?

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: Ɛ ba gyeŋ nyi na ɔ konɔɔ man te ŋee gyu ka gyoo yee? Ŋee gyu bo ka gyoo ɔ dubulu man, na ta lee ɔ wose man.” Yesu ke yako kei nɛ, na ɔ nawolo baa nyi weenɛɛ kamasɛ dei na kedi.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Nombiakumɛɛ kɛŋa pou na lee bo walaŋ konɔɔ man, te ŋan ne yɛɛ na e ayimɛɛ.” Siria alo ŋolo ke lɛɛ Yesu di Mateo

ref: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
text: Keŋte alo ŋon mɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ayi, me Gbeŋgyoo, doo mena. Mɔ babaase baa tale fiu weenɛɛ ŋan bia ne dii te ŋɛɛ yale tɛɛle man nɛ di.”

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Lee botɔɔ te Yesu korowɔ gyu Tae na Sidɔn donɔɔse ŋan ŋe yɛɛ Iseraetena donɔɔse ŋa nɛ yenaŋ. Te ɔ ka gyoowɔ dekpaŋalaŋ daale man keŋ ɔ te gyae nyi ŋolo ka gyeŋ nyi ɔ kyaa botɔɔ ya, mɔna balaŋ naa e.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Te alo ŋolo ŋon feliŋkum doo ɔ bu alebubii man ka betaa nyii nyi Yesu kyaa botɔɔ nɛ, te ɔ kaa buŋawɔ ɔ nawɔɔ tɛɛ.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: Na alo ŋonaŋ yɛɛ Giriki alo e, te ba lola e bo Fonisia keŋ ke kyaa Siria tɛɛle man nɛ. Keŋte ɔ sola Yesu nyi waa gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee ɔ bu ŋon man.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse e nyiaa, “Nyi n dana bia na, n ke sɛɛ mo be weenɛɛ fa babaase? Koa, n ke taŋgbɛɛ mo weenɛɛ ŋan fa bia kedi pɛ. Te akpaa be dii kaase na, pɛna na n ka mo fa babaase.”

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Alo ŋon ka lee botɔɔ gyu ɔ dɛɛ nɛ, te ɔ kenaawɔ nyi ɔ bu ŋon doo mpa dɔɔ na feliŋkum keŋ ta lee ɔ man. Yesu kekyɔ omumuu ŋolo kawee Mateo

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Yesu ka lee Tae donɔɔ man nɛ, ɔ tɛɛwɔ Sidɔn donɔɔ man ka lee Galelia boo nɔɔ nɔɔ kɔŋ donɔɔse kufu ŋaale tɛɛle man.

ref: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
text: Keŋte balaŋ baale moo balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ deŋela be tɔɔ te ɔ bɛɛ tale kolosi nideli ya nɛ kɔŋ Yesu gyaŋ. Te ba sola e nyi waa mo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ, na waa kyɔ e ɔ kawee.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu moo kaweete ŋon lee balaŋ baŋ man gyu yenaŋ. Te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaasebia do kaweete ŋon deŋela man, te ɔ tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ mo tina ɔ laakem.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Te Yesu kɛɛ adido te ɔ lee weesɛɛ te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Efata!” Keŋ nawolo nyi toro!

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ deŋela torowɔ te ɔ laakem booliwɔ, te ɔ talewɔ kolosi nideli.

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Keŋte Yesu faree balaŋ baŋ nideli nyi be na yako walaŋ kamasɛ ya. Mɔna keŋ na ɔ ke faree wɔ koraŋ nɛ, keŋ gba te be yela balaŋ nyiiwɔ beiŋ.

ref: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
text: Balaŋ baŋ wose yeŋawɔ nideli te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Yesu te yɛɛ kpene kamasɛ nideli. Ɔ te yela deŋela atɔɔsatena ne nyii dei, te omumuuse mɔ ne kolosi.”

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: Yesu ka toro nombia kɛŋa yako fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nɛ, te Pita moo e lee yenaŋ te ɔ faree e nyi ɔ na kolosi mena ya.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia dana maŋ waraŋase. Nawolo nyi gyɛŋ weeya kpaakpaa atooro yaa ŋgba be kyaa me gyaŋ, te kolo be doo baa di ya nɛ.

ref: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
text: Nyi akpaa me lɔ wɔ gbɛɛ na tanam na, be sia gyae kaa biri wɔ gbɛɛnaa man. Nawolo nyi be man baale lee lemlem”

ref: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
text: Te Yesu yela balaŋ baŋ kyakeewɔ tɛɛle man, te ɔ moo bodobodose nyetooro ŋan te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa mo fa ɔ kaseela baŋ nyi baa mo kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ. Keŋte baŋ mɔ moo ŋa kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.

ref: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
text: Na ɔ kaseela baŋ dana kpebia kyomii baale mɔ. Te Yesu moo kpebia baŋ mɔ te ɔ fanewɔ fa Wurubuarɛ karaŋ te ɔ moo ŋa fa wɔ nyi baa mo kpɛlɛɛ fa balaŋ baŋ.

ref: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
text: te ŋon na ɔ kaseela baŋ gyoowɔ degbele man gyu Dalimanuta tɛɛle man. Farasiitena ne gyae nyi Yesu waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia wola wɔ Mateo

ref: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
text: Keŋte Farasiitena baale kɔŋawɔ Yesu gyaŋ bɛɛ bɔɔse e nombia na baa mo teese ɔ nɔɔ man. Be yako e nyi waa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na kawola nyi ɔ lee Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ ampaŋ.

ref: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text: Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ kee gyu nɛ, na ɔ kaseela baŋ te wolee be ta mo bodobodo ŋaale tela kpu na dokoloŋ keŋ doo ba degbele keŋ man nɛ ya.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
text: Yesu ka gyeŋ kpene keŋ bɛɛ yako nɛ te ɔ bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te ɛ ne kolosi nyi ɛ ta mo bodobodo tela ya? Mena dɔɔ ɛ ta gyeŋa ta nombia man yee? Ya agyueŋse man ta toro ta yee?

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu kyaŋa siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ moo e lee donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ woli ɔ sia te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N ne naa kolo?”

ref: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
text: Keŋte balee ŋon gyinaa ɔ sia kɛɛ adido te ɔ yakowɔ nyiaa, “Mɛɛ naa balaŋ, mɔna be yɛɛ ŋgba daase ne tɛɛ la nɛ.”

ref: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
text: Te Yesu besewɔ mo ɔ nyiŋmaa tina balee ŋon sia bela. Keŋte ɔ sia gyuusuwɔ, te ɔ naa kpene kamasɛ nideli.

ref: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu lɔɔ balee ŋon gbɛɛ te ɔ faree e nyiaa, “Na bese gyu donɔɔ keŋ man bela ya. Ta gyu n dɛɛ.” Pita kpa Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Mateo

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
text: Keŋte Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ mɔ de? Ɛ kpa maŋ yɛna amɔ?” Botɔɔ te Pita tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Nyaŋ yɛna Kristo, nyeelɛɛre ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ”

ref: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nyi be na yako ɔ wose man nombia fa ŋolo na ŋolo ya. Yesu kekolosi lee ɔ yeŋ na ɔ kefoŋ wose man Mateo

ref: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
text: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu fiasɛɛ ɔ na wola ɔ kaseela baŋ nombia nyiaa, “Kaboena nyi maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa naa diyem nideli, na Gyudatena kegyiise na Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ke bɛɛ maŋ nyi na maŋ yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon ya. Baa ko maŋ, mɔ weeya atoorote keŋ na maa foŋ lee yeŋ man.”

ref: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ ɔ kaseela baŋ te ɔ fae Pita nyiaa, “Ta lee me gyaŋ, ɔbɔnsam! Na Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ ya, mɔna denibalaŋ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ.”

ref: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Keŋte Yesu baake balaŋ dikpii keŋ na ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Akpaa ŋolo ne gyae waa sila maŋ na ɔ na yɛɛ kpene keŋ ŋon gbagba ne gyae ya. Mɔna waa mo ɔ ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele, na waa sila maŋ.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
text: Nawolo nyi walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa lɛɛ ŋon gbagba nyeedoŋ na, ke gyae ka lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man. Te walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man lee maŋ Yesu na me nombia dɔɔ nɛ, waa bese nyiŋ ke.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself?
text: Akpaa ŋolo de nyiŋ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ abɔɔ pou te ɔ nyeedoŋ da lee ɔ nyiŋmaa man na, woŋ tɔnɔɔ te waa nyiŋ?

ref: What could you give to get back your soul?
text: Yaa woŋ te walaŋ waa tale mo kyɛɛkee na ɔ nyeedoŋ?

ref: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
text: Akpaa maŋ Yesu na me nombia de yɛɛ ŋolo desɛŋ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ besena be wɔle tee Wurubuarɛ te be yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ nɛ siaman na, kenaŋ na wee keŋ maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ daa na me kya Wurubuarɛ na Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ gyoorobiiri man nɛ, walaŋ ŋonaŋ mɔ nombia gyae ke yɛɛ maŋ desɛŋ.”

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Mɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, Elia taŋ kekɔŋ te balaŋ yɛɛ e kpene kamasɛ keŋ be gyae ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see be yako lee ɔ wose man nɛ.” Yesu kekyɔ tobaale ŋon ɔ na wee keleuleuse nɛ Mateo

ref: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
text: Kenaŋ wɔle Yesu ka gyoo deni man nɛ, ɔ kaseela baŋ bɔɔse e deni man nyiaa, “Woŋ dɔɔ te daa te tale gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee bu ŋon man ya?”

ref: Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said,
text: Botɔɔ te ɔ moo biikyaabii ŋolo kaa seŋ ba nsana, te ɔ pukaa e te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa,

ref: Then Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
text: Keŋte Mosesi na Elia kaa bɛɛwɔ lee be dɔɔ, te be kolosiwɔ na Yesu.

ref: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Botɔɔ te ŋɔma kaa wuu be dɔɔ, te be nyii nɔɔwoya ŋaale ŋɔma keŋ man nyiaa, “Kei yɛna me bu ŋon mɛɛ gyae e. Ɛ nyii e.”

ref: At once the disciples looked around, but they saw only Jesus.
text: Debokenaŋ man ɔ kaseela batooro baŋ besewɔ kɛɛ na, ba te naa walaŋ kamasɛ kpu na Yesu ya.

ref: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
text: Mena dɔɔ nombia ŋan dɔɔwɔ ba nyeeya man na bɛɛ bɔɔse dɔŋa nyiaa, kefoŋ lee yeŋ man kei na wolo baa nyi sena?

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Keŋte be bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera dɔɔ te Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ ne yako nyi, kaboena nyi Elia waa kyaŋ gbɛɛ kɔŋ pɛna Kristo ŋon Wurubuarɛ be yako see nyi waa kɔŋ nɛ ke kɔŋ?”

ref: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
text: Ba ke kɔŋ ɔ kaseela akaŋ baŋ gyaŋ nɛ, be naa balaŋ burum te yilaa kilisi wɔ. Na Gyudatena mmaraa wolala baŋ, na ɔ kaseela baŋ ne wɔŋee nombia ŋaale.

ref: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
text: Mena dɔɔ ba moo tobaale ŋon kɔna Yesu. Feliŋkum keŋ kena Yesu nɛ, debokenaŋ man te ke yela bu ŋon keŋawɔ ŋgba keleuleuse kyaŋa na e nɛ. Bu ŋon leewɔ yala tɛɛle man, na ɔ ne biliŋi gyu kɔŋ, na afofose na lee ɔ nɔɔman.

ref: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
text: “Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ lɛɛ biikyaabii kei dinɔɔ ma yele man na ɔ te lɛɛ maŋ, te ŋon ɔ lɛɛ maŋ na ɔ te lɛɛ ŋon ɔ be kpila maŋ nɛ.” Balee ŋolo moo Yesu yele ɔ ne gegi feliŋkumɛɛ Luka

ref: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
text: Balaŋ baŋ pou ke gyɔ sibii naa Yesu nɛ, doo be nɔɔ te be yeuwɔ ke gyaŋee na e te ba doo e nɔɔ.

ref: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ao! Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ Wurubuarɛ di ya. Weeya aŋmoŋ yaa ɛ ne gyae nyi maa nyiŋ konɔɔ kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, pɛna ɛ ke lɛɛ maŋ di? Ɛ mo bu ŋon kɔna maŋ.”

ref: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse bu ŋon ɔ kya nyiaa, “Weeya aŋmoŋ yaa ŋgba kawee kei ba gyoo e nɛ?” Keŋte balee ŋon tiranɔɔ nyiaa, “Lee naŋ ɔ biatɛɛ.

ref: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
text: Debokenaŋ man te bu ŋon ɔ kya fae yeesa yako nyiaa, “Mɛɛ lɛɛ di. Kyɔ maŋ na maa lɛɛ neŋ di kpu nideli.”
